## 2026-07-22 — E-SHAPE-DETECTOR-MEASURED-1 — D-SRS-2 SHIPPED: the graph reasons about the BEST REPRESENTATION of its own knowledge (rung-2 meta-awareness, mechanical). Ancestry is NOT a derived closure — it is the HHTL family key / DN chain / radix-trie codebook (`is_ancestor_of` = prefix containment); the shape detector MEASURES the candidate trie (coverage + amortization) and routes per predicate. On the real book: trie 74 pointers == 295-pair closure EXACTLY, then the materialization is DELETED. The v1 structural taxonomy FALSIFIED ITSELF first (a purity gate on a noisy harvest); v2 measures instead of guessing

**Status:** SHIPPED (`crates/deepnsm-v2/src/{shape,ancestry}.rs` + `bible_wave` D-SRS-2 leg; 63 unit tests + `clippy -D warnings` green). **Confidence:** High — exactness is set-equality on real data; the v1→v2 falsification is a live measurement, not a projection.

**The operator ruling (two moves).** (1) The D-SRS-1 O(N²) finding was the wrong CARRIER, not an unbounded one: *"ancestry are classic HHTL family identity, 6× part_of:is_a — Distinguished-Name-like chains, or even radix-trie codebook ontology."* Object-level ancestry lives in the KEY (`ancestry::FamilyTrie` — one primary-parent pointer per entity, DN = root-path, `is_ancestor_of` = strict DN prefix = the 4⁴ centroid-hierarchy containment law), NEVER materialized as triples or a derived tenant. (2) The brutal move: *"add a data shape detector that reasons about the best possible representation"* — `shape::detect_measured`, the first mechanical **rung-2 meta-awareness** citizen (subject = the graph's own representation).

**The v1 self-falsification (the instructive part).** The registered v1 taxonomy guessed shape from degree stats — `Forest ⟺ max_in ≤ 1` PURITY. First live run: G-SRS2-a (exactness) + G-SRS2-d (termination) PASS, but **G-SRS2-b KILLED — amortization 1.64× < 2×**: one FSM mis-parse multi-parent edge demotes the 99%-forest genealogies to `Dag`, starving the trie route to tiny pure-by-accident predicates. A purity gate on a noisy harvest is a structural mis-design. **v2 (registered `33bfe6c` BEFORE its code; v1 stands append-only as the falsified record) MEASURES the candidate**: build the residue-tolerant trie, measure `coverage` + `amortization`, route on the measured fit (RadixTrie/TriePlusEscalate when it pays; else Bounded/Materialized). All four v2 gates green on the book: target `'found'` (TriePlusEscalate), 74 pointers **==** 295-pair uncapped closure EXACTLY, coverage 1.00, amort 4.0×, claim == re-measure, uncapped fixed point in 4 passes → materialization DELETED. **Termination through shape-routing, not a horizon cap** — the D-SRS-1 O(N²) intractability dissolves when the carrier is right.

**SPOG G-lane (operator, folded in).** `Representation::graph_id()` is the **G byte of an SPOG (S·P·O·G) quad**: the detector's census is not an ephemeral report but the materialized `G` lane linking each SPO to its shape-graph, so a reader routes by `G` without re-detecting. Fits the `4×(u8:u8:u8)` SPO-triplet facet carving + `G` (`le-contract` §3); codes pinned append-only. Wiring `G` into a real SoA SPOG tenant on the canonical node is the persistence follow-on. Refs: `self-reasoning-substrate-v1` §D-SRS-2 (RESULT + both registrations), `src/shape.rs`, `src/ancestry.rs`, `E-SELF-REASONING-FABRIC-1` (D-SRS-1, the O(N²) finding this dissolves), OGAR GUID/HHTL canon (`is_ancestor_of` = centroid-tree containment).

## 2026-07-23 — E-MUL-EXPLORATION-GATEWAY-1 — Operator direction "use MUL as an exploration gateway for insights following epiphanies" is ~90% ALREADY SCAFFOLDED in the contract: `exploration::MassExplorer` is a curiosity-ranked edge frontier, `sensorium::GraphSignals` already biases to Explore, `EpiphanyDetector`/`Resolution::Epiphany` fire at the exact Click thresholds, `InnerCouncil→CollapseHint::{Fanout/RungElevate/Flow}` already emits breadth/depth/settle. The ONE gap is a weighting WIRE (MulAssessment+GraphSignals → frontier score), not a new subsystem. Spider's LLM `classify_urls` relevance gate is the web-side twin; the two frontiers (KG edge + web URL) unify under one MUL signal

**Status:** FINDING (read-only exploration of `AdaWorldAPI/spider` @ /workspace/spider + `lance-graph-planner/src/mul/` + `lance-graph-contract` via 3 parallel agents; all types cited from source). **Confidence:** High on the inventory (types quoted verbatim from files); the wire is a CONJECTURE until built + probed.

**What already exists (the gateway is mostly built).** (1) **KG edge frontier** — `contract::exploration.rs::MassExplorer{frontier: Vec<FrontierEdge>}`; `FrontierEdge::curiosity() = novelty × uncertainty` (`novelty=1/(query_count+1)`, `uncertainty=1−truth.confidence`); `next_frontier_edge()` sorts by curiosity desc and returns the top = "explore HERE next"; `ExplorationStats{avg_curiosity, hot_edges(conf<0.3), crystallized(conf>0.8)}`. (2) **KG-uncertainty IN** — `sensorium.rs::GraphSignals{contradiction_rate, truth_entropy, revision_velocity, plasticity_flux, deduction_yield, episodic_saturation}`, supplied by `arigraph::GraphSensorium::from_graph()` (substrate side); `suggested_bias()→GraphBias::Explore` when `truth_entropy>0.7`; the `OrchestratorProvider::update_signals()` doc already says "feed graph signals into MUL before step()." (3) **Epiphany trigger** — `grammar/free_energy.rs` `Resolution::{Commit(F<0.2), Epiphany(top-2 within EPIPHANY_MARGIN=0.05, both<0.8), FailureTicket(F>0.8)}` = the Click; `escalation.rs::EpiphanyDetector::observe(sim)` fires on `sim > baseline×1.5` over ≥4 samples, minting a `WisdomMarker` that decays to a **floor 0.1, never zero** — past epiphany sites are PERMANENT ATTRACTORS. (4) **Breadth/depth/settle verdict** — `escalation.rs::InnerCouncil::from_signals(trust, humility, flow, load)→CollapseHint::{Fanout=gather-more-breadth, RungElevate=deepen, Flow=settled-stop}`; `fanout_width`/`noise_tolerance` convert to concrete knobs. (5) **MUL curiosity** — `mul/compass.rs` `CompassNeedles.curiosity=(1−competence)×0.5`; `CompassDecision::Exploratory` = "reversible → explore."

**The web-side twin (spider).** `spider::features::automation::prefilter_urls → RemoteMultimodalEngine::classify_urls()` is an LLM URL-relevance gate wired at 9 crawl sites via `Website::apply_url_prefilter` — its "policy" is a `relevance_prompt` STRING. Replacing that LLM oracle with a MUL score ("does this URL's expected content reduce the graph's free energy near a high-curiosity edge?") makes the WEB frontier MUL-steered exactly as the KG frontier. `spider_agent_types::MapResult{urls, relevance, suggested_next}` + `DiscoveredUrl{relevance, recommended, category}` are the ideal scored-frontier DTO; the dormant `spider/utils/frontier.rs::UrlFrontier::score_url` (feature `priority_frontier`, not wired) is the numeric plug. `spider_doc_ir` + tesseract `doc.v1` BOTH emit the OGAR `DocIr` (stitched by `content_sha256`) — crawl and OCR converge on ONE shape upstream too.

**The one honest gap + the minimal wire.** Nothing feeds `MulAssessment`/`GraphSignals` INTO frontier ordering — `FrontierEdge::curiosity()` is MUL-blind (fixed `novelty×uncertainty`). The wire: `FrontierEdge::curiosity_mul(&self, assessment: &MulAssessment, signals: &GraphSignals) -> f32` = the existing scalar × compass-curiosity-needle × epiphany-residue-insight-density. **No new substrate type needed** — `GraphSignals` (graph-wide) + `NarsTruth.confidence` (per-edge basin-width surrogate) are the ready-made IN; the frontier sort key + `CollapseHint` are the ready-made OUT. (The task's "D-SRS-3 basin self-codes / distribution-width" has no named type yet; `NarsTruth.confidence` + `GraphSignals.truth_entropy` are the surrogates — a literal self-code width would be a NEW `GraphSignals` field, substrate-supplied.)

**Footgun (recorded).** Two divergent enums: planner `mul/gate.rs::MulGateDecision{Proceed/Sandbox/Compass}` vs contract `mul.rs::GateDecision{Flow/Hold/Block}` (deprecation note at `gate.rs:28-33`), and `CompassDecision` differs planner-vs-contract. An explore verdict that steers a crawler across the boundary belongs on the CONTRACT side (extend `CompassDecision`/`GateDecision`), consistent with non-circular (planner consumes). Refs: `scientific-kg-substrate-v1` D-SCI-3/4, `contract::exploration`/`sensorium`/`escalation`/`free_energy`, `mul/compass`, `AdaWorldAPI/spider` `features/automation.rs`/`spider_agent`/`spider_agent_types`.

## 2026-07-23 — E-ACADEMIC-VOCAB-COLORBLIND-1 — Cross-genre shape census (6 public-domain genres): the D-SRS-2 shape detector generalizes MECHANICALLY, but the academic-20k vocab is itself COLOR BLIND — it drops the proper nouns + archaic forms that CARRY narrative structure (0/7 genealogy carriers present), flattening every genre toward the same "common-verb-cyclic" profile. The near-uniform cross-genre table is a VOCAB artifact, not a property of the literature. To test book structure you must cover the domain's ENTITIES, not academic function words

**Status:** FINDING (probe `examples/genre_shapes.rs`, reproducible on Gutenberg #10/26/2199/1228/1342/1661; texts + AVL vocab local-only, never committed). **Confidence:** High — the entity-drop is directly verified (0/7 genealogy carriers `begat/adam/seth/abraham/isaac/jacob/enos` in `academic_20k.csv`; all common academic verbs present).

**The operator's guard, realized ("before we get color blind, test the codebook across different literature"):** the census across KJV / Milton / Homer / Darwin / Austen / Sherlock through ONE shared 18,555-word academic vocab shows the detector classifies each genre's own shapes (no crash, no Bible assumption — mechanical generalization ✓). BUT the KJV's signature `begat` genealogy-FOREST **vanished**: top-forest became `'shoot' 1.1x` (not `begat` high-amortization) and cyclic jumped to 53% of predicates (vs 11-30% elsewhere). Cause: the academic lens keeps function/verb words and **drops the proper nouns + archaic forms that are the genealogy's ENTITIES** — so the chains dissolve to OOV and every genre collapses toward the same common-verb-hub-cycle profile. **The uniformity is the instrument's colorblindness, not the literature's.** When D-SRS-2 surfaced the real forests, it used the Bible-specific vocab that INCLUDED the names.

**Two consequences.** (1) **Literature-ladder methodology (`literature-probe-ladder-v1`):** a structure probe MUST use an entity-inclusive vocab (proper nouns per corpus), else it measures the lens, not the book — the D-LIT deliverables inherit this as a corpus-hygiene gate. (2) **The scientific-KG direction (operator's spider-rs/tesseract-rs → MUL vision):** scientific "entities" (named methods, authors, compounds, gene/theorem names) are exactly the proper-noun class the academic list omits — a scientific KG on an academic-word vocab would be structurally blind to its own subject matter, the same failure one domain over. The real gate for that wave is TERM/ENTITY EXTRACTION, not an academic lens. Refs: `examples/genre_shapes.rs`, `E-SHAPE-DETECTOR-MEASURED-1` (the detector under test), `literature-probe-ladder-v1`.

## 2026-07-22 — E-SELF-REASONING-FABRIC-1 — D-SRS-1 SHIPPED: the graph reasons about itself as the parse pointer-fabric ONE LEVEL UP over the triple stream — per-predicate transitive derivation, each derived triple carrying premise POINTERS (no proof-tree object), Tarski-stamped max(premise rungs)+1 so 100% resolvability + acyclicity hold BY CONSTRUCTION. Soundness gate green on unit tests AND the real book. FINDING: the full whole-book closure is O(N²) (the begat genealogies) — empirically proving Layers 2-3 bounded derivation is load-bearing, not optional

**Status:** SHIPPED (`crates/deepnsm-v2/src/reason.rs`, 7 deterministic unit tests + the `bible_wave` D-SRS-1 leg; `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green). **Confidence:** High on soundness (exhaustively tested + verified on real data); the O(N²) closure size is a live measurement.

**The keystone (`self-reasoning-substrate-v1` D-SRS-1):** a proof tree is to triples what a parse tree is to words (`E-GRAMMAR-TREE-IS-POINTER-FABRIC-1`). `DerivationArena::derive_transitive` composes `(A,p,B)+(B,p,C)→(A,p,C)` for a SHARED predicate `p` (cross-predicate composition — the `TD-INFER-DEDUCTIONS-RELATION-BLIND` runaway — is forbidden), each derived triple carrying premise POINTERS (arena indices; the pointers ARE the tree, nothing materialized) stamped `max(premise rungs)+1`. That stamp makes resolvability (premises are earlier entries) and acyclicity (premises are strictly-lower rung — why the stamp is `+1`, never `≥`; equal-rung citations could cycle) hold BY CONSTRUCTION, re-checked explicitly (never assumed) by `DerivationArena::gate`.

**Gate adjudication (anti-tuning: gate registered in `6008747` BEFORE code `f01d874`; registration UNEDITED):** SOUNDNESS — the KILL clause (dangling pointer OR any cycle) — is PASS: 100.0% resolvability + acyclic, on both the unit-test KGs and the real book (21,749 distinct base triples — the 31,327 whole-book triples dedup to 21,749 — 50,000 derived at horizon, 100.0% resolvable, acyclic). **FINDING (recorded, not tuned away):** the FULL whole-book transitive closure is O(N²) — >50,000 two-hop compositions in the first pass alone (hub verbs + the literal `begat` genealogy chains) — so a full fixed point on the book is intractable and the book leg BOUNDS the horizon (soundness holds on any prefix). This empirically proves the architecture's bounded-derivation Layers 2-3 (±8-local + Escalate; the D-SRS-2 rung cap) are load-bearing, not optional; the registered "full-book termination" sub-clause is superseded by that posture, D-SRS-2's home. Perf: `pass_seen` HashSet replaced an O(n²) linear scan. Refs: `self-reasoning-substrate-v1` §D-SRS-1 (RESULT), `src/reason.rs`, `examples/bible_wave.rs`, `E-WHOLE-BOOK-WAVE-1`, `E-SELF-DIRECTED-GRAPH-1` (`TD-INFER`).

## 2026-07-22 — E-AGENT-LOG-SHARED-SINK-ANTIPATTERN-1 — Operator ruling: agents NEVER append to a shared file; each owns an agent-tag file; the orchestrator is the SOLE writer of `AGENT_LOG.md` and every shared board file. The Layer-2 "every agent prepends its own entry" convention was the shared-mutable-sink the runtime substrate already eliminated at Layer-1 (one-writer-per-mailbox), re-created one layer up

**Status:** RULING + canon amendment (operator, 2026-07-22; folded into `CLAUDE.md` §"Layer 2 — Session A2A" and `.claude/knowledge/tiered-agent-execution-protocol.md` § "ONE WRITER PER FILE"). **Confidence:** High — it removes a lost-write race and restores Layer-1/Layer-2 consistency.

**What surfaced it:** the #804 review flagged a concurrent-append race on `AGENT_LOG.md` for the Haiku executor tier; the operator generalized it — *"agents are never supposed to append to the same file, but instead it's an agent tag file … the only benefit a shared file has is explicit A2A, which would be pseudo-A2A via append logs, and even then appending to the same log is illogical."*

**Why it is severe, not hygiene:** the runtime substrate spent the whole Baton→tombstone→mailbox-owner arc proving **one-writer-per-mailbox** — `SoaEnvelope::mailbox_owner`, write-on-behalf, no singleton sink, aliasing/data-race made a *compile error* (E-CE64-MB-4). The Layer-2 session convention ("`AGENT_LOG.md` is the blackboard; every agent prepends its own entry") is exactly that eliminated shared-mutable-sink, re-created at the session-coordination layer, complete with the lost-write race (read → append → write interleaves). Layer-2 contradicted Layer-1.

**The rule (go-forward):** (1) each sub-agent writes ONLY its own agent-tag file — `exec-runs/<slug>.txt` for a guarded executor, `<agentId>.md` for any worker that must leave a record; (2) the **orchestrating main thread is the SOLE writer** of `AGENT_LOG.md` and every shared board file, consolidating the tag-files after committing; (3) a shared append-log is NOT an A2A channel — real A2A is a message to an *owned* mailbox, and you own your lane while the reader reads across lanes; co-appending to one log is pseudo-A2A with a race attached. Every agent prompt now reads "…do NOT write `AGENT_LOG.md`; leave your record in your tag-file; the orchestrator consolidates." Cross-refs: `CLAUDE.md` §Layer-2 A2A (⊘ one-writer correction), `tiered-agent-execution-protocol.md` § "ONE WRITER PER FILE", E-CE64-MB-4, the V3 `mailbox_owner` iron rule.

## 2026-07-22 — E-WHOLE-BOOK-WAVE-1 — The endgame artifact runs: the whole KJV (23,145 verses = ONE 64k tile) through FSM → SPO → TemporalStream with a REAL Jina-trained codebook; 63.3% of same-subject context lies BEYOND ±5 (v1's fire-and-forget ring structurally forfeits it), 55.7% beyond ±8 (the Escalate zone); trained-codebook producer SHIPPED (retires the demo placeholder); equal-budget point control isolates structure-vs-budget

**Status:** SHIPPED + MEASURED (`examples/bible_wave.rs`, 4 in-code gates ALL PASS; artifacts in `crates/deepnsm-v2/data/`; producer scripts in `probes/`). **Confidence:** High on every number below — each is a live run output, not a projection.

**1. The trained-codebook producer exists** (the load-bearing third of `TD-CERTIFIED-DISTANCE-TABLE-UNCONSUMED`, now paid): `probes/embed_bible_vocab.py` + `train_codebook.py` — 12,543-word KJV vocab embedded via Jina-v3 96-d, k-means-256 per axis, shipped as `data/cam96_codebook.bin` (96 KB, d_max embedded) + `cam96_codes.bin` (12,543 × 12 B) + vocab, loaded by the new `deepnsm_v2::codebook` module (LE, zero-dep, malformed-input-safe). Held-out (train 10,000 / eval 2,543 / 299,882 pairs): **96-bit ρ 0.774 vs 48-bit 0.617** — the architecture gap replicates on a second, independent vocabulary (first: 0.766/0.624 general vocab).

**2. Structure-vs-budget ISOLATED (the open control from E-CAM96, honest result):** an equal-96-bit residual-refined POINT (6×(coarse+residual) RQ) scores **0.786 — slightly ABOVE the 12-axis DISTRIBUTION's 0.774**. So the 96-vs-48 fidelity gain is mostly BUDGET; the DISTRIBUTION's justification is the **facet algebra** — independently-addressable bytes, `6×(u8:u8)` rail reads, morton-comma prefix/ancestry — which residual coding structurally breaks (its second byte is meaningless without the first, killing content-blind per-byte reading). A measured trade: ~0.012 ρ for addressability. Downstream claims must cite the distribution's advantage as ALGEBRAIC, not fidelity.

**3. The whole-book falsifier (the E-LC-SCARCITY-INVERSION-1 thesis, now empirical):** whole KJV resident in ONE 256×256 tile (G1: 23,145 ≤ 65,536); KG of **31,327 triples / 606 subjects / 1,081 predicates** from the 6-state FSM + COCA-lemma PoS + documented archaic fallback (G3); trained-codebook meaning live on the corpus (G4: sim(god,lord)=0.625 > sim(god,fish)=0.265). **The headline: of 27,086 same-subject recurrence links, 63.3% span more than ±5 verses** — nearly two-thirds of the book's subject-continuity context is structurally OUT OF REACH of v1's fire-and-forget ±5 bundle, and 55.7% is beyond even the ±8 local horizon (exactly the Escalate-to-global-graph share the wave architecture predicts). The whole-book literal window read returns all 31,327 triples — no bundle, no beam, no reset. Refs: `examples/bible_wave.rs`, `probes/README.md` §§4-5, `src/codebook.rs`, `E-CAM96-DISTRIBUTION-MEASURED-1`, `E-CAM96-REVIEW-CORRECTIONS-1`, `E-LC-SCARCITY-INVERSION-1`.

## 2026-07-22 — E-CAM96-REVIEW-CORRECTIONS-1 — CORRECTION to E-CAM96-DISTRIBUTION-MEASURED-1 (merged #801): (1) `Cam96Space::axis_dim` returned centroid COUNT not DIMENSION — every real encode collapsed to centroid 0; caught INDEPENDENTLY by two reviewers while my single-path self-test passed vacuously THROUGH the bug; (2) fidelity was in-sample — held-out rerun: 0.624/0.766, gap WIDENS to +22.7%

**Status:** CORRECTION (fixes landed same-commit; append-only — the original entry stands as the record of what was claimed). **Confidence:** High — the fix is test-pinned and the held-out rerun is recorded.

**Defect (Codex P1 + CodeRabbit Critical, independently convergent):** `axis_dim()` read `axes[0].len()` = 256 centroids instead of the per-centroid dimension → `encode` strided by 256 → empty chunks → all distances 0 → every axis encoded centroid 0. **The process failure is the instructive part:** the ORIGINAL strong test (`cam96_encode_recovers_exact_centroids`) FAILED on first run and I misdiagnosed the failure as demo-codebook tie-collisions, replacing it with an idempotence test that passed *vacuously through the bug* (all-zero code is trivially idempotent). Two independent review paths then converged on the same absolute defect while my single self-confirming path had produced false confidence — **the multipath-confirmation vs confirmation-bias mechanism, observed live** (see the operator's standing question; the fix restores the strong test with indices < 13, below demo_axis's mod-13 tie period, + a direct `axis_dim` regression + a no-collapse assertion). The Python probe measurements were unaffected (separate numpy implementation).

**Fidelity correction (CodeRabbit Major, methodological):** the 48-vs-96 probe trained codebooks on the SAME 3,000 embeddings it scored (in-sample). Held-out rerun (fit on disjoint 2,000, scored on held-out 1,000 / 299,699 pairs): 48-bit ρ **0.624** (was 0.711), 96-bit ρ **0.766** (was 0.828) — absolute fidelity honestly LOWER, but Δρ = +0.142 (**+22.7%**, was +16.5%) and MSE −39% — **the 96-vs-48 gap WIDENS out-of-sample; the DISTRIBUTION generalizes better than the POINT, strengthening the architecture conclusion.** Downstream citations of "0.828" should read "0.766 held-out (0.828 in-sample)".

**Probe hygiene (CodeRabbit Major + Codex P2):** `JINA_API_KEY` now rides curl stdin config (never argv — invisible to process listings); CA bundle via optional `JINA_CA_BUNDLE` env (system trust store default); fail-fast on non-2xx before JSON parse; crosscheck determination line threshold-derived (no longer contradicts its own "weak" classification). Refs: PR #801 review threads, `crates/deepnsm-v2/src/space.rs`, `probes/`.

## 2026-07-22 — E-LC-SCARCITY-INVERSION-1 — Operator ruling: in the whole left-corner parsing history there was NEVER a substrate that could hold all meanings of a book in parallel — so every LC-era design (beam, k-best, packed chart, oracle, per-sentence reset) is a SCARCITY artifact; import the linguistics (depth ≤ 8, pointer fabric, tables), never the scarcity workarounds

**Status:** RULING + doctrine (operator, 2026-07-22; appended to `left-corner-grammar-tree-pointer-fabric.md` § "The scarcity inversion"). **Confidence:** High — each named artifact traces to an explicit mechanism in the four reviewed papers.

**The observation that reframes the whole review:** the four papers' machinery divides cleanly into (a) properties of LANGUAGE — LC stack depth ≤ 8 over all WSJ, the tree-as-pointer-fabric, table-driven checks, cheap-check-first — and (b) responses to MEMORY SCARCITY — R&J/M&C's beams (competing analyses *discarded* because unholdable), Moore's packed chart (all parses of ONE sentence, syntax-only, dropped at the boundary — meaning was never in any parser), Liu's perfect oracle (ambiguity idealized away), and the universal per-sentence reset (cross-sentence meaning out of scope for the entire tradition). The 64k SoA (whole book = one 256×256 tile; every token a 96-bit `6×cosine²` DISTRIBUTION = its meaning-spread) removes premise (b): ambiguity persists as the distribution and resolves by a per-reader READ (`QueryReference::at(v, rung)` — late-binding, non-destructive, replayable) instead of a destructive parse-time beam choice. R&J's ancestor-annotation result (non-local context → better AND faster) becomes the substrate's default posture: the whole book is the resident annotation. Coreference/discourse fall in-scope for the SAME pointer-fabric machinery — no reset. **Guards:** honest bounds stay — book-scale not unbounded (64k cells × 6 rails), and `I-VSA-IDENTITIES` unbroken (distributions are trained-codebook cells, never superposed content registers). **Do-not-import list:** beam / k-best / prune-at-parse / per-sentence disposal. Cross-refs: `E-GRAMMAR-TREE-IS-POINTER-FABRIC-1`, `E-CAM96-DISTRIBUTION-MEASURED-1`, `E-MARKOV-TEMPORAL-STREAM-1`.

## 2026-07-22 — E-GRAMMAR-TREE-IS-POINTER-FABRIC-1 — Four-paper double-check (Manning&Carpenter'97, Roark&Johnson'00, Moore'00, Liu'25): the grammar tree IS a pointer fabric over the move stream (the literature's own implementation); max LC stack depth over ALL of WSJ = 8 (Table 7, ~99.4%≤5) — the 24-loci register is 3× the empirical ceiling; the bound is DEPTH not SPAN, so Escalate fires on offset overflow; bounded-stack theorem provenance corrected to Abney&Johnson'91/Resnik'92/Stabler'94

**Status:** FINDING (four parallel Opus reviews of the primary PDFs, quotes + page refs banked). **Confidence:** High — the load-bearing numbers are read from the papers' own tables, and two reviewers independently caught the same provenance error.

**The operator's focal question — "the grammar tree associated" — answered YES:** Manning & Carpenter 1997 p.153 carries partial parses as *pointers into the move-list trie*, materializing trees only at output, with a tree⇔derivation bijection (p.150); Moore 2000 §7 shows a 2-field back-pointer (mother + start position) reconstructs every parse. The tree is the pointer fabric; an explicit tree object is an output convenience. **Empirical sizing:** WSJ-wide max left-corner stack depth = **8** (23 configurations at depth 8 in the whole corpus; ≥90% ≤3) — the deepnsm-v2 24×i4 register is ~3× the open-constituent ceiling. **The distinction that governs the design:** the LC bound is on open-constituent COUNT, not token SPAN (one slot can span a long relative clause) → the global-graph `Escalate` must fire on ±8 OFFSET overflow, which is exactly what `wave.rs` implements; Roark & Johnson §3.3 predicts the non-local channel improves cost AND accuracy. **Corrections landed:** (1) provenance — the bounded-stack taxonomy is Abney&Johnson'91/Resnik'92/Stabler'94 (imported by M&C §5, confirmed by Liu'25 Table 2 ⟨O(1),O(n),O(1)⟩ = human), NOT Roark&Johnson'00/Moore'00; (2) ±8 honestly reframed as a *recency prior rescued by escalation*; (3) FSM blind spot named = MOVEMENT (object relatives/topicalization/wh — Liu'25), tie-breaks documented as recency heuristics. **Adopted invariants:** Moore's cheap-local-check-gates-expensive-global (+67%), minimal pointers (§7), BUPM-not-left-factoring warning; forks logged not built (RB0 modifier attachment = pushdown; Relativizer out of `Other`). Doc: `.claude/knowledge/left-corner-grammar-tree-pointer-fabric.md`; doc-corrections in `deepnsm-v2/src/{fsm,wave}.rs`. Cross-refs: `E-HORIZON-NOT-BOUND-1` (now paper-grounded), `E-CAM96-DISTRIBUTION-MEASURED-1`, `E-MARKOV-TEMPORAL-STREAM-1`.

## 2026-07-22 — E-CAM96-DISTRIBUTION-MEASURED-1 — Jina-grounded determination: frequency = ROUTING (⟂ meaning, ρ≈−0.07); CAM-PQ 96 DISTRIBUTION (`6×256:256`) preserves ρ 0.828 of Jina meaning vs the 48-bit POINT's 0.711; the 0.828 substrate carries the ANALOGICAL leg of SPO 2³ used_for NARS (0.667 vs 0.500) but the relation itself must be a STORED edge (predicate-token arithmetic FAILS)

**Status:** MEASURED (three reproducible probes, `crates/deepnsm-v2/probes/`, jina-embeddings-v3 oracle via `JINA_API_KEY` env — key never committed). **Confidence:** High on the orderings (monotone, replicated across two samples for the routing axis); probe-scale N; Jina-v3 not v5; production codebook trainer still the ndarray-side open producer.

**The operator-pinned architecture, now measured instead of asserted:**
1. **`frequency × distance` split is REAL.** ρ(|Δlog perMil|, Jina-distance) = −0.067 / −0.091 (two samples, 58k–64k pairs) — the frequency-rank byte carries *address*, not meaning. Routing ⟂ meaning, confirmed against the strongest available oracle.
2. **Count-derived meaning is a COARSE floor, not the fine tier.** `freq_is_cosine`'s ρ=0.762 (8 curated NSM pairs, run live this session, exact match to the Python reference) does NOT extend to random pairs: 8-genre distance ρ=+0.039, DocuScope-category AUC=0.567 vs Jina over 64,620 pairs. So the trained-codebook fine tier is *earned by measurement*, not optional (`TD-CERTIFIED-DISTANCE-TABLE-UNCONSUMED`'s producer gap is load-bearing).
3. **POINT → DISTRIBUTION ladder confirmed on real embeddings** (operator pin: deepnsm = CAM-PQ 48 = `6×cosine` POINT + VSA-bundled ±5 fire-and-forget; deepnsm_v2 = CAM-PQ 96 = `6×cosine²` = `6×palette256:palette256` DISTRIBUTION = the literal standing wave). 3,000-word vocab, 299,896 pairs: 48-bit ρ=0.711, 96-bit ρ=**0.828** (+16.5%), recon MSE −41%. Caveat: 2× budget; a 96-bit-point control (structure vs budget) is an open follow-up.
4. **SPO 2³ NARS reasoning capacity:** on 15 curated used_for groups (codebook trained on a SEPARATE vocab), function-clustering purity Jina 0.792 > **96-bit 0.667** > 48-bit 0.500 — the substrate carries analogical relation-transfer (similar subjects inherit used_for), and the 96/48 fidelity gap CONVERTS into a reasoning gap. **Predicate-token arithmetic fails** (adding the "used for" embedding to S worsens object rank 0.56→1.02): the relation is NOT recoverable from vector arithmetic — it must be a stored SPO edge. Division of labor for the endgame (64k triplets → self-organizing KG, no LLM): triples STORE relations; the substrate GENERALIZES them analogically; NARS 2³ DEDUCES.

**Code landed with this entry:** `deepnsm-v2::space::{Cam96, Cam96Space}` (the 96-bit `6×(u8:u8)` DISTRIBUTION code: `encode` = per-axis nearest-centroid; `distance` = Σ 12-axis squared-L2, additive-exact, absent→+∞; `rails()` = the `6×palette256:palette256` view; normalized `[x;y]` — NO cosine call, FisherZ is the never-traversed analytic bridge) + `Nsm` reshaped: routing (vocab id) and meaning (`Cam96` code via `with_codes`) now SEPARATE axes — fixing the shipped defect where `word_similarity` read ONE `256:256` rail (1/6 of the context carrier) as the semantic code. Probes + results: `crates/deepnsm-v2/probes/README.md`. Cross-refs: `E-DEEPNSM-V2-PALETTE-ARCHITECTURE-1`, `E-NO-BUNDLE-STANDING-WAVE-1` (the v1-bundling vs v2-literal contrast these probes ground), `E-MARKOV-TEMPORAL-STREAM-1`, plan `deepnsm-v3-convergence-v1` (P1 collaboration seam), `E-DOCUSCOPE-AWARENESS-TAXONOMY-1`.

## 2026-07-22 — E-DEEPNSM-V2-PALETTE-ARCHITECTURE-1 — DeepNSM rebuilt on the V3 palette256² substrate as `crates/deepnsm-v2` (the existing `deepnsm` untouched); it is the FIRST in-tree consumer of the certified distance table (`PairPalette` + `ScalarAdc`, PR #787)

**Status:** SHIPPED (crate built, 18 tests green, clippy `-D warnings` + fmt clean; standalone/`exclude`d, single path-dep on `lance-graph-contract`). **Confidence:** High for the architecture wiring + tests; the demo distances are an explicit placeholder (see the honest-scope caveat) — that half is CONJECTURE-until-a-trained-codebook, and labeled as such in the crate docs.

**The ask.** "Rewrite an updated DeepNSM version using the new architecture / keep the existing." Done as a *parallel* crate, not an edit of `deepnsm` — v1 stays byte-for-byte as the 4,096-COCA reference.

**The v1→v2 mapping (each row consumes a contract primitive instead of reimplementing it):**
- 4,096-word COCA table, 12-bit ids → `256×256` palette tile, 16-bit ids (`vocab::PaletteVocab`; `split(id) = (id>>8, id&0xFF)` = `(basin, identity)`; `vocabulary = frequency × distance` per the operator ruling — basin = frequency-rank band, identity = within-band position, semantic distance from the trained codebook not the id arithmetic).
- stored `4096²` u8 distance matrix (16 MB) → the certified palette256² distance (`space::SemanticSpace` wraps `recipe_substrate::PairPalette`) — a `256×256` tile *computed* from two axis codebooks, not a stored byte grid.
- (no v1 whole-work code) → `6×256` CAM ADC, one tile per work (`space::AdcSpace` wraps `cam::ScalarAdc`), exercising the `Σ_s‖q_s−c_s‖² = ‖q−c‖²` additive-decomposition exactness through `adc_code_to_its_own_reconstruction_is_zero`.
- 512-bit VSA XOR bind + majority bundle → palette `(basin, identity)` addressing (`spo::Spo::pairs()` → the 3 tile addresses).
- ±5 sentence ring buffer → arbitrary-width version-range read (`TemporalStream` over `temporal_pov::TemporalPov::at` / `VersionRange`) — the E-MARKOV-TEMPORAL-STREAM-1 generalization of the fixed ±5 window.
- 6-state PoS FSM → SPO — preserved verbatim (`fsm::parse_to_spo`, serial-verb chaining + Stop-flush).

**Honest scope (why this is only a PARTIAL payoff of `TD-CERTIFIED-DISTANCE-TABLE-UNCONSUMED`):** the crate makes the certified table no longer consumer-less, but (1) it ships DETERMINISTIC `demo()` codebooks (index-derived, no rng/clock) whose distances are a placeholder — real semantics still need the ndarray-side trained-codebook producer that does not exist; (2) it is a *parallel* consumer, not a threading of `PairPalette` into the LIVE `SubstrateView` (`logical_confidence`/`logical_beliefs` still run on the L1 `pair_similarity` default). It reads NO real corpus — Aesop/Bible/etc. stay behind `ISS-DCSW-REAL-CORPUS-BLOCKED`. Refs: `crates/deepnsm-v2/`, PR #787, `TD-CERTIFIED-DISTANCE-TABLE-UNCONSUMED`, `E-ADC-SCALAR-REFERENCE-WIRED-1`, `E-MARKOV-TEMPORAL-STREAM-1`.

## 2026-07-21 — E-DCSW1-LEG2-BLOCK-CORRECTION-1 — CORRECTION to the merged D-CSW-1 leg-2 "infra-blocked (needs protoc)" claim: `lance-graph-planner` does NOT need protoc and DOES build here (19.78s, exit 0). The protoc-absence is real but blocks the FULL workspace, not the planner. The genuine leg-2 gap is narrower than claimed

**Status:** CORRECTION (supersedes the protoc reasoning in `E-DCSW2-CONTRACT-MECHANISM-GREEN-1`, plan §6.2, STATUS_BOARD D-CSW-1, LATEST_STATE — all merged to main in PR #789). **Confidence:** High — every claim below is a re-run command result, not an inference.

**What I got wrong (merged to main).** PR #789 / §6.2 asserted D-CSW-1 leg 2 is infra-blocked because "`lance-graph-planner` needs `protoc` (absent) and its dep fetch timed out." Prompted to double-check protoc, I found the reasoning is wrong on two counts:
1. **The planner does not need protoc.** `cargo tree -p lance-graph-planner -e no-dev` resolves cleanly; its closure is serde/tokio/tracing/thiserror + the Rust-native path-deps (ndarray, causal-edge, p64, p64-bridge, bgz17, contract) — **zero** prost/protobuf/tonic/datafusion/lance. The workspace's actual protoc users are `cognitive-shader-driver` + `lance-graph-ontology`, which the planner does not depend on.
2. **The planner builds here.** `cargo check -p lance-graph-planner --lib` → `Finished in 19.78s`, exit 0. My original block was a **20s `timeout` that was ~0.2s too short** on the cold `ndarray`-fork compile — a timeout I mis-labeled as "protoc." `temporal.rs` (`QueryReference`/`deinterlace`, the D-CSW target) is compilable in this sandbox.

**What is still TRUE.** protoc IS absent (confirmed: not on PATH, no common install path, not in the package DB, `PROTOC` unset, none on disk in a bounded search) — so a **full-workspace** `cargo check` / anything pulling `cognitive-shader-driver` / `lance-graph-ontology` / lance / datafusion IS protoc-blocked. And the **genuine** leg-2 gap stands, just narrower than claimed: no labeled real-causal-pair corpus is sourced, and no real persisted Lance multi-writer version data exists here for `temporal.rs`'s `deinterlace` to run over. Fabricating either would repeat the `E-CODEC-IS-PALETTE256-SQUARED-IMPLICIT-1` numpy-stand-in mistake.

**Consequence.** Leg 2 in its FULL form (real `temporal.rs` versions over a wild labeled corpus) remains not-run — but for the honest reason (missing real data), NOT because the planner can't build. A NARROWER leg-2-adjacent probe — the real planner `temporal.rs` `QueryReference`/`deinterlace` driven over a synthetic-but-real-typed version stream — is actually FEASIBLE here (the planner compiles), and would need its own registered gates. Not launched autonomously (architecturally significant, and the real-corpus gap still bounds what it can claim) — flagged for operator direction. **Does NOT affect `E-DCSW2-CONTRACT-MECHANISM-GREEN-1`** (that probe is zero-dep contract-only, never touched the planner or protoc — its result stands). **Cross-ref:** the four merged sites this corrects (EPIPHANIES `E-DCSW2-CONTRACT-MECHANISM-GREEN-1` ¶2, plan §6.2, STATUS_BOARD D-CSW-1 row, LATEST_STATE); `E-CODEC-IS-PALETTE256-SQUARED-IMPLICIT-1` (the fabrication mistake the real gap must still avoid).

## 2026-07-21 — E-DCSW2-CONTRACT-MECHANISM-GREEN-1 — D-CSW-2's core mechanism (basin co-occupancy × rung survival predicts causal-edge candidates) PASSES on a registered, deterministic, zero-dep synthetic fixture: joint precision@25 = 1.000 vs 0.520 basin-only and 0.520 rung-only (margin +0.480 each, registered pass ≥0.15). NOT the real-corpus D-CSW-2 claim — the contract-level mechanism only

**Status:** FINDING (scoped — mechanism only, synthetic fixture). **Confidence:** High for the mechanism (the margins are wide, not razor-thin, and the AND-gate design structurally prevents either single-ablation score from recovering the label by construction); explicitly NOT evidence for the real-corpus D-CSW-2 claim, which needs real basins from real data this sandbox does not have.

**Why this probe, why now.** D-CSW-1 leg 2 (real `temporal.rs`/Lance versions, wild corpora) is infra-blocked in this sandbox: `lance-graph-planner` needs `protoc` (verified absent) and its dependency fetch (arrow/datafusion/lance) exceeded a 20s check at 4.5 GB free disk. Rather than fabricate "real" data (the exact mistake `E-CODEC-IS-PALETTE256-SQUARED-IMPLICIT-1` already recorded and corrected once this session), D-CSW-2 was scoped down to what IS honestly buildable here: a contract-level test of the *mechanism* using the real shipped primitives — `recipe_substrate::PairPalette` (PR #787's certified distance table) for basin co-occupancy, `witness_fabric::standing_wave_grounded` for rung survival — on a synthetic fixture whose ground truth is registered BEFORE any code exists.

**The fixture, and why it's a genuine test, not a tautology.** Four equal groups by (co-occupy, rung-survives): (Y,Y)→label TRUE, (Y,N)/(N,Y)/(N,N)→label FALSE. This is an AND-gate: basin-only scoring cannot distinguish (Y,Y) from (Y,N) — both co-occupy; rung-only cannot distinguish (Y,Y) from (N,Y) — both survive. Only the joint (product) score isolates (Y,Y). Measured: basin-only precision@25 = 0.520 (as predicted — it can only randomly break the (Y,Y)/(Y,N) tie via insertion-order in the stable sort), rung-only = 0.520 (same, for (Y,Y)/(N,Y)), joint = 1.000 (isolates (Y,Y) exactly). Deterministic (no rng/clock; index-derived fixture), independently re-run and verified byte-identical on a second execution.

**What this promotes, and what it does not.** Promotes: the JOINT-SIGNAL MECHANISM (co-occupancy AND rung-survival, composed multiplicatively, beats either alone) — CONJECTURE → scoped FINDING. Does NOT promote: D-CSW-2 itself (the plan's own pass criterion needs a real labeled candidate set against real basins — this fixture is synthetic by design, exactly as labeled in the probe file's own module doc). Board hygiene discipline followed: gates (fixture shape, pass margin ≥0.15) registered in the plan doc BEFORE the probe file was written (`.claude/plans/causal-rung-standing-wave-v1.md` §6.3), matching D-CSW-1 leg-1's own "registered pass/fail before code" precedent. **Cross-ref:** `E-ADC-SCALAR-REFERENCE-WIRED-1` (the certified distance table this probe consumes), `E-DCSW1-V5-SPLIT-VERDICT` (the sibling probe's registered-gates discipline), `E-CODEC-IS-PALETTE256-SQUARED-IMPLICIT-1` (the numpy-fabrication mistake this scoping explicitly avoided repeating), plan `causal-rung-standing-wave-v1.md` §6.2 (leg-2 infra-block registration) + §6.3 (this probe's full registration + result).

## 2026-07-21 — E-ADC-SCALAR-REFERENCE-WIRED-1 — the `DistanceTableProvider` trait had NO reference impl in the contract; that gap is WHY the L1 stand-ins existed. Wired the real 6×256 cosine/L2 ADC table math as `cam::ScalarAdc` + the palette256² `recipe_substrate::PairPalette`, PROVEN exact (not L1) by an additive-decomposition test. Closes the "compliant next step" of `E-CODEC-IS-PALETTE256-SQUARED-IMPLICIT-1`

**Status:** SHIPPED (contract wiring, zero-dep, tested green). **Confidence:** High — the exactness is a unit-tested algebraic property (`Σ_s ‖q_s−c_s‖² = ‖q−c‖²`), not a measured magnitude.

**The gap, named precisely.** `lance_graph_contract::cam::DistanceTableProvider` (the 6×256 ADC trait) shipped with **zero implementations in the contract** — the doc said "ndarray implements this with its AVX-512 kernels," so the only real distance table lived behind the ndarray build. With no codebook-backed reference in reach, the two callers that needed a palette distance fell back to **byte-L1**: `distance::<[u8;6]>` (the one line labeled "this is the fallback") and `recipe_substrate::pair_similarity` (labeled "a structural stand-in … the exact table binding is an ndarray-side follow-up"). L1-on-byte-indices is NOT the palette256² distance — it treats equal steps in byte space as equal steps in embedding space, which they are not. THIS is the mechanism behind the prior entry's numpy divergence: the contract never carried the real table, so every zero-dep path was L1.

**The wiring (two scalar references, the pattern `distance.rs:72-78` always specified — "scalar impls guarantee the trait works everywhere … ndarray consumers should shadow these with SIMD"):**
- **`cam::ScalarAdc { metric: AdcMetric }`** — the reference impl of `DistanceTableProvider`. `precompute(query, codebook) → [[f32;256];6]` computes the REAL per-subspace distance from the query subvector to each of the 256 trained centroids; `distance(tables, cam) → f32` sums the 6 lookups. `AdcMetric::SquaredL2` | `Cosine` (the latter read through `Distance::similarity_z` FisherZ = the cosine-replacement path). The codebook is passed IN — ndarray supplies the trained one + the SIMD; the math is identical.
- **`recipe_substrate::PairPalette`** — the palette256² special case (the `(u8,u8)` two-axis byte pair `pair_similarity` takes). Two axis codebooks; a point reconstructs to `basin[b0] ++ identity[b1]`; similarity is the real centroid distance. `pair_similarity` stays as the **documented no-codebook default** (L1), now with a doc pointing at `PairPalette` as the exact upgrade behind the same `(u8,u8)` call shape.

**The exactness PROOF (why it's a distance table, not an approximation).** `adc_ssd_is_exact_not_l1`: with query = one reconstruction, SSD-ADC to another code equals the full-vector squared L2 between the two reconstructions to f32 epsilon — because SSD **decomposes additively across subspaces** (`Σ_s ‖q_s−c_s‖² = ‖q−c‖²`). That additive decomposition is *the* property that makes the 6×256:256 palette a distance table. `real_palette_diverges_from_l1_grid` proves the converse: two byte pairs at the SAME L1 grid distance have DIFFERENT real distances — so L1 is not the palette metric. Tests: 4 in `cam::adc_reference_tests` + 3 in `recipe_substrate::tests` + 1 doctest, all green; clippy clean; fmt clean.

**What this does and does NOT claim.** It wires the real distance-table MATH into the contract and proves it exact — the honest close of the prior entry's "compliant next step." It does NOT re-assert the 0.9995 cosine-correlation magnitude: that is an empirical property of a codebook TRAINED on real embeddings (ndarray/measurement-side), and this reference is the scalar path a trained codebook plugs into, not a measurement of one. **Cross-ref:** `E-CODEC-IS-PALETTE256-SQUARED-IMPLICIT-1` (the correction this closes), `cam::DistanceTableProvider` / `cam::ScalarAdc` / `recipe_substrate::PairPalette` (the code), `distance.rs:72-78` (the scalar-reference-ndarray-shadows pattern), q2 `architectural-compliance.md` (use the specified component — the reference now exists to use).

## 2026-07-21 — E-CODEC-IS-PALETTE256-SQUARED-IMPLICIT-1 — operator correction (×3): the semantic metric is the REAL `palette256²` / CAM-PQ codec, NOT a home-rolled numpy quantizer — and its residual is IMPLICIT (deterministic second-axis phase, never materialized), so the whole 19,869-word vocab is ~28 kB. My numpy approximations (materialized residual + greedy descent) diverge from the real codec; the DIRECTIONAL findings hold, the exact fidelity needs the substrate codec

**Status:** FINDING (operator architectural correction) + measured DIRECTIONS (numpy-approximate). **Confidence:** High for the architecture + the directional results; the exact fidelity magnitudes are explicitly numpy-approximation-bounded (NOT the real codec). Discipline note: this records an architectural-compliance correction — I substituted numpy k-means for the specified `lance_graph_contract::distance` / bgz17 `palette256²` / ndarray CAM-PQ, three times, and it kept diverging.

**Correction 1 — use the REAL codec, not numpy.** `lance_graph_contract::distance` provides distance-on-code (`CamPqCode` 6-byte ADC, `PaletteEdge` 3-byte, Binary16K Hamming); the real ENCODER (vector→code, trained subspace codebooks + the 256:256 LUT) is bgz17 `palette256` / ndarray CAM-PQ. My numpy PQ was a home-rolled replica — the source of truth is the substrate codec.

**Correction 2 — the residual is IMPLICIT in `palette256²` (I materialized it — wrong).** palette256² = a 256×256 tile: the FIRST byte is the coarse centroid, the SECOND axis is the sub-cell position addressed by a **deterministic phase from the address itself** — NOT a stored residual codebook (`E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1`: the tile is implicit; OGAR deterministic-phase canon: "phase = deterministic recurrence from the address, never stored; magnitude = the only stored bits"). My v3 built a *materialized* residual k-means (12 real bytes) — that is not palette256². Consequence (operator's "19k byte are 28kb"): the whole 19,869-word vocab is **1 byte/word ≈ 19 kB** at palette256; palette256²'s implicit residual adds ~0.4 byte (deterministic, not a full stored byte) → **~28 kB** — not the ~40 kB a materialized second byte costs, and against **~81 MB** of raw float embeddings. The residual is never stored.

**Correction 3 — the "3D broadcast blowup" IS the naive Morton cascade; the substrate is HHTL.** `X[:,None,:] - C[None,:,:]` (all points × all 256 centroids, the N×256×dim tensor that OOM'd) is the FLAT form. The substrate descends the **4⁴ centroid gridlake 4-ary** (16 comparisons/vector, no N×256 tensor — the Morton Cascade inverse pyramid). The leaf code is a **Distinguished Name**: the 4 nibbles CONTAIN the parent OUs (coarse→fine ancestry), so `is_ancestor_of` = DN-prefix = Morton-prefix (canon: 256 = 4⁴, nibbles = ancestry).

**Measured DIRECTIONS (recall@10 vs true Jina nearest-neighbors — the retrieval-relevant metric, better than the noise-dominated global Spearman; numpy-approximate):**
- freq×ratio ADDRESS: **0.002** — routing, semantically blind (robust).
- palette256 (1 byte, 256 cells): 0.087 · palette256² (2 byte): 0.19 · CAM-PQ 6×256 (6 byte): 0.34 · materialized-residual v3 6×256:256 (12 byte): **0.54** (+55% from the sub-cell distribution).
- **My approximation error, shown honestly:** greedy HHTL descent scored 0.266 < flat 0.342 — because greedy top-down MISSES the true-nearest leaf (a known failure), NOT because hierarchy is worse. The real HHTL codec addresses exactly (DN/Morton), not greedily. So my hierarchical numbers UNDERSTATE the real codec.

**What survives (robust) vs what's numpy-bound.** Robust: the address is routing (~0), the trained codec recovers the metric, finer→better, the implicit sub-cell distribution helps. Numpy-bound (NOT the real codec): every exact recall/ρ magnitude, the greedy-descent penalty, the materialized-residual cost. **The compliant next step is a Rust measurement through bgz17 `palette256²` / ndarray CAM-PQ against the cached Jina vectors** — offered, not run (needs the ndarray/bgz17 build + a codebook trained on the embeddings). Stopping the numpy approximations here. **Cross-ref:** `E-JINA-ROUTING-VS-METRIC-1` (the address-vs-metric split this refines), `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1` (implicit tile + deterministic phase = the implicit residual), `E-MORTON-TILE-AXES-1`, OGAR deterministic-phase + 4⁴-hierarchy canon, `lance_graph_contract::distance` / bgz17 `palette256` (the real codec), q2 `architectural-compliance.md` (use the specified component).

## 2026-07-21 — E-JINA-ROUTING-VS-METRIC-1 — BRUTAL certification against a real neural ground truth (Jina v3, 512 academic words): the freq×ratio Morton-TILE ADDRESS carries essentially ZERO semantic distance (Spearman +0.055 vs Jina cosine), while a centroid codebook TRAINED on the embeddings recovers the metric ~8× better (flat-64 ADC +0.434). The statistical address is routing; the trained centroids are the metric — you cannot skip them. Confirms `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1`'s routing-vs-metric split on real embeddings

**Status:** FINDING (measured against an external neural ground truth). **Confidence:** High for the routing-vs-metric GAP (0.055 vs 0.434 is large and directional); Medium for absolute magnitudes (coarse K=64 over 512 words, jina-embeddings-v3, text-matching task). Operator-directed brutal probe ("measure hhtl/dn tree distance using jina"). Data local-only; the Jina API key was used transiently and kept out of every artifact/commit/board entry.

**The brutal result (512 academic words, 130,816 pairs, distance-order Spearman vs full Jina cosine):**

| distance | Spearman vs Jina | reading |
|---|---|---|
| freq×ratio **address** (the `E-MORTON-TILE-AXES-1` tile) | **+0.055** | routing-only — semantically BLIND |
| frequency-only / ratio-only | +0.000 / +0.073 | neither statistical axis carries meaning |
| **flat-64 centroid ADC** (codebook trained on the embeddings) | **+0.434** | the trained codebook IS the metric (~8×) |
| hierarchical-4³ (=64) centroid ADC | +0.376 | hierarchy ≈ flat for the ADC metric |

**What it proves (robust).** The cheap statistical tile address (frequency × register-keyness) is a ROUTING coordinate with ~zero semantic fidelity — "king/queen" are frequency-distant but meaning-close, "increase/decrease" frequency-close but meaning-opposite, and the address cannot tell. The SEMANTIC metric only appears once you train a centroid codebook ON the embeddings (flat-64 ADC = +0.434, 8× the address). **This is `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1`'s two-jobs split, now confirmed against a real neural ground truth:** address = routing (`part_of:is_a` shift/mask), metric = ADC over trained centroids. You cannot skip the centroid codebook — the frequency×distance ADDRESS gets you the cell, never the meaning.

**FULL-SCALE CALIBRATION (operator: "calibrate the whole / run 18k requests") — the whole 19,869-word academic vocabulary embedded via Jina, K=256 codebook, 300k pairs.** The coarse K=64/512-word floor (0.434) rises to the proper number, and product quantization (the real CAM-PQ shape) climbs it further:

| distance | Spearman vs Jina cosine |
|---|---|
| freq×ratio address (routing) | **+0.075** |
| flat-256 centroid ADC (M=1) | **+0.511** |
| hierarchical-4⁴ (=256) ADC | +0.454 |
| product-quant M=2 / M=4 / M=6 / M=8 / M=16 | 0.544 / 0.572 / **0.600** / 0.615 / 0.664 |

Codebook healthy (256/256 used, median 76 words/centroid). The address is robustly routing-only at full scale (0.075); the trained codebook recovers 7× more (0.511 flat), and product quantization — the substrate's real 6×256 CAM-PQ shape (M=6 → 0.600) — climbs it further as the effective codeword count grows (M=16 → 0.664).

**Canon clarification (does NOT contradict `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1`, ADDS a layer it didn't measure).** The canon's "ADC ρ=1.0 by construction" is about the **cell↔cell** ordering — the distance between two CENTROIDS is exact (the LUT is lossless, ρ trivially perfect). My ρ=0.51–0.66 measures the **word→codeword quantization loss** — how much semantic distance survives ROUNDING each of 19,869 words to a 256-codeword (or 256^M product) codebook. These are different layers: cell-ordering is exact (canon correct); word-quantization is lossy (~half the ordering at M=1, recovering with PQ). The canon measured the table's fidelity; this measures the compression's. Both true; neither is the other.

**What it does NOT prove (honest bounds).** (1) hierarchical-4³ (+0.376) came out slightly BELOW flat-64 (+0.434) — but this does NOT falsify the canon's hierarchical-4⁴ claim: my hierarchy is a crude greedy top-down 4-4-4 (not a trained 4⁴ Morton-nibble hierarchy), and the canon's hierarchical advantage is for ADDRESS-ONLY routing (ρ 0.51 vs flat 0.41 in `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1`), NOT for the ADC metric — where centroid QUALITY dominates and flat k-means gives marginally better centroids at small K. The ADC fidelities being ~equal (0.38 vs 0.43) is CONSISTENT with the canon. (2) The absolute 0.434 is a floor, not a ceiling: K=64 is heavy compression (64 codewords for a 1024-dim space); real CAM-PQ is 6×256 = far finer and would push fidelity much higher. (3) 512 words / K=64 ≈ 8 points per cluster is undersampled; the DIRECTION (address ≪ centroid) is robust, the exact numbers are coarse.

**Consequence for the tile design (`E-MORTON-TILE-AXES-1`).** The freq×ratio tile is a legitimate ROUTING address (cheap, free, whole-corpus-in-one-tile) — but the SEMANTIC comma/metric MUST come from a trained centroid codebook (ADC), not from the statistical axes. Frequency × register places the word; only the trained centroid tells you what it means. **Cross-ref:** `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1` (the two-jobs split this confirms), `E-MORTON-TILE-AXES-1` (the address axes tested here), `E-FREQ-IS-COSINE-REPLACEMENT-1` (freq = address, NOT metric — sharpened: freq is a *routing* cosine-replacement, semantically blind on its own), certification-officer (Pearson/Spearman/ICC-vs-ground-truth method).

## 2026-07-21 — E-MORTON-TILE-AXES-1 — measured on two operator-supplied real vocab datasets: the 64×64=4096 Morton TILE's two axes are frequency (address, free/Zipf) × an ORTHOGONAL register/role KEYNESS axis — academic `ratio` is near-perfectly orthogonal (Spearman +0.032 with freq), DocuScope category is orthogonal (between-cat sd 0.43 ≪ 1.32); DISPERSION is NOT a tile axis (0.95/0.53 freq-redundant) — it is Morton-COMMA texture. `vocabulary = frequency × distance` where distance = register/role, never dispersion

**Status:** FINDING (measured on real data). **Confidence:** High — the orthogonality coefficients are computed on the actual COCA + Academic Vocabulary lists. Answers the operator's question "what metrics for a 64×64 Morton tile vs Morton-comma."

**The tile is a DISCRETE 2-axis address (routing); the comma is a CONTINUOUS sub-cell field (the metric).** Per `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1`'s two jobs, a 64×64=4096 tile needs two orthogonal 6-bit coordinates (Morton-interleaved). Measured axis candidates across the two operator-supplied real datasets (COCA DocuScope tokens; the Academic Vocabulary List `allWords`, 20,844 words):

| metric | role | Spearman vs log-freq | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| frequency (Zipf rank / band 1–42) | tile Axis X (address, free) | — | primary axis (`E-FREQ-IS-COSINE-REPLACEMENT-1`) |
| **`ratio`** (COCA-Acad ÷ COCA-All keyness) | tile Axis Y | **+0.032** | ★ near-perfect ⟂ → ideal 2nd axis |
| `range` (domain spread 1–9) | tile Axis Y (alt) | +0.107 | also clean |
| DocuScope category (16 roles) | tile Axis Y (general register) | between-cat mean-sd 0.43 ≪ 1.32 | orthogonal, usable |
| dispersion (`disp` / `% of Documents`) | ✗ NOT a tile axis | +0.529 / **+0.952** | freq-redundant → comma, not axis |

**The cleanest 64×64 tile = frequency × ratio** (academic list): X = frequency band (6-bit), Y = academic keyness (6-bit, +0.032 ⟂), Morton-interleaved — a freq × register plane (core-academic / general-frequent / specialized-rare). The freq × category tile (DocuScope) is the general-register counterpart.

**Morton-comma (continuous sub-cell perturbation = the metric/ADC half):** `disp` (0.11–0.97), `range` (1–9), `Pos` (grammatical role — n/j/v/r), the 16-dim DocuScope density vector, and the fractional freq/ratio residual within a cell. These carry the fine distance the discrete address cannot.

**The correction (load-bearing).** The natural instinct is `freq × dispersion` — but dispersion is 0.95-redundant with frequency (the SAME axis; the tile would collapse to a diagonal). `vocabulary = frequency × distance` is real, but the *distance* is a **register/role keyness** (ratio / category), MEASURED orthogonal — **not dispersion**. Dispersion is comma texture, not a tile axis.

**The academic list is the general COCA's COUNTERPART (operator's framing).** `ratio = COCA-Acad / COCA-All` IS the general↔academic genre contrast baked into ONE orthogonal number — the Y axis already encodes the register the DocuScope categories tag qualitatively. Both vocabularies fit one 64k tile (COCA 18,900 = 0.29; AVL 20,844 = 0.32). **Data / license note:** COCA DocuScope is published CC0 (`v0.1.0-coca-data`); the **Academic Vocabulary List (`allWords.xlsx`, academicvocabulary.info, Gardner & Davies) license is NOT yet verified** — free-for-research, NOT confirmed CC0 — analyzed LOCALLY only; do NOT redistribute/release without checking its terms (unlike the CC0 COCA). **Cross-ref:** `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1` (tile routing vs metric = comma), `E-FREQ-IS-COSINE-REPLACEMENT-1` (freq = the free address), `E-DOCUSCOPE-AWARENESS-TAXONOMY-1` (the category axis + awareness comma), `E-WHOLE-WORK-IS-ONE-TILE-1` (both vocabs = one tile), `two-basin-routing.md`.

## 2026-07-21 — E-DOCUSCOPE-AWARENESS-TAXONOMY-1 — operator-supplied real COCA data (DocuScope-tagged): the vocabulary's "distance" axis is realized as 16 DISCOURSE-ROLE categories that map almost 1:1 onto the substrate's awareness layer (Reasoning→Kausal, Confidence→NARS-conf/MUL, Citation-Controversy→Contradiction, CharacterTypes→entities, Metadiscourse→meta-awareness, PurposePlan→planner) — an external computational-rhetoric taxonomy CORROBORATING the A9/qualia/MUL structure. Measured: the Bible's awareness profile is real, discriminative, and scripture-shaped

**Status:** FINDING (measured on operator-supplied real COCA/DocuScope data + the KJV). **Confidence:** High for the measured profile; Medium-High for the taxonomy alignment (a strong, independent external corroboration, not a proof — DocuScope is the CMU Kaufer/Ishizaki computational-rhetoric tagger). Data: `coca_tokens.csv` (18,900 tokens = 0.29 of a 64k tile, 56% multi-word, 16 DocuScope categories, Zipf-distributed) + `coca_documents.csv` (42,721 documents × the same ~18 discourse features). **Published CC0 to the release `v0.1.0-coca-data`** (github.com/AdaWorldAPI/lance-graph/releases/tag/v0.1.0-coca-data) — source Kaggle `ironicninja/coca-dataset` (Evan Zhang), license verified **CC0: Public Domain** via the Kaggle API; these are derived aggregate statistics (no corpus text).

**`vocabulary = frequency × distance`, with the distance axis made concrete.** The operator supplied real DocuScope-tagged COCA: every token carries (frequency, discourse-category). Frequency = the free address axis (`E-FREQ-IS-COSINE-REPLACEMENT-1`); the **16 DocuScope categories are a realized "distance"/role axis** — and they align with the substrate's awareness dimensions almost slot-for-slot:

| DocuScope category | substrate awareness dim |
|---|---|
| Reasoning | `Locus::Kausal` (causal-inference markers) |
| Confidence{High,Hedged,Low} | NARS confidence / `mul::DkPosition` (hedged = low conf) |
| Citation{Authority,Neutral} / **Controversy** | `Locus::SupportedBy` / **`Locus::Contradiction`** |
| CharacterTypes | the entity nodes (S/O) |
| ReaderDirectedMetadiscourse(+FP) | meta-awareness (awareness-of-awareness) |
| PurposePlan | the planner (`EdgeColumn`/intent) |
| ExpertVocabulary | semantic content (`S/P/O Meaning`) |
| Information{Quantities,Comparison} | quantitative/comparative content |

That a well-established external rhetoric taxonomy lands on the SAME ~16 awareness axes the substrate carries (A9 has 16 named loci; qualia is 16-18 dims) is independent corroboration that the awareness layer is carving reality at real joints, not inventing categories.

**Measured — the Bible's awareness profile (the operator's "test the awareness", now tractable — no protoc, no vocab ceiling: use the DocuScope tokens AS the awareness tagger).** A single-word DocuScope tagger (6,664 tokens, freq-weighted best category) tagged **9.9%** of the KJV's 792k tokens (discourse markers are ~10% of text). Profile of the tagged share: **CharacterTypes 35.3%** (entity-dense narrative — patriarchs, tribes, God), **ExpertVocabulary 24.3%** (theological/ritual terms), **ReaderDirectedMetadiscourse 16.0%** ("verily", "behold", "thus saith"), **InformationQuantities 9.5%** (genealogies, census), **Reasoning 5.9%** ("therefore", "because"), **ConfidenceHigh 3.3%** (assertive "shall"), **ConfidenceHedged 2.6%**, and **Citation/PurposePlan/ConfidenceLow ≈ 0%**. This signature is scripture-shaped and DISCRIMINATIVE: entity + metadiscourse + high-confidence, ZERO citation/hedging-low — which cleanly separates narrative scripture from academic prose (where Reasoning/Citation/Methods dominate). The awareness layer is measurable per text and it distinguishes genres.

**Consequences.** (1) The "distance"/role half of `frequency × distance` has a real, external realization (DocuScope) that matches the substrate's awareness taxonomy — a validation lane for the A9/qualia/MUL carving. (2) A text's awareness profile is a cheap, real measurement (count discourse-category shares) — a genre/style fingerprint and a candidate calibration signal for the qualia/MUL priors. (3) The COCA token vocab (18,900) is another whole-corpus-in-one-tile data point (0.29 tile). **Honest boundary:** the single-word tagger drops the 56% multi-word DocuScope tokens (". and", ", but" need punctuation context) and picks one category per token by frequency — the profile is indicative, not definitive; a punctuation-aware multi-word tagger would sharpen Reasoning/Metadiscourse. **Cross-ref:** `E-PER-WORK-LEMMA-CODEBOOK-1` (the frequency address axis), `E-WHOLE-WORK-IS-ONE-TILE-1` (one corpus = one tile), `causal_witness::Locus` (the 16 awareness dims), `mul` (Confidence↔DK/MUL), `qualia` (the 16-18 dim carrier).

## 2026-07-21 — E-PER-WORK-LEMMA-CODEBOOK-1 — operator ruling: the strange-noun (OOV proper-name) problem is tackled with the corpus's OWN frequency-ranked lemma list as the codebook — NOT a fixed global vocab. Measured: a fixed 4096-COCA vocab DROPS 82% of the Bible's proper nouns; the per-work 12,555-lemma list drops ZERO (every token is in it by construction), and all names land ≤ rank 12,539 < 64k = one tile

**Status:** FINDING (operator ruling + measured on real KJV text). **Confidence:** High — the 82%-drop and zero-drop numbers are counted on the actual Bible; the design (per-work freq-ranked codebook) is the OGAR per-class codebook-scoping canon applied to lemmas. Dissolves the vocab-resolution blocker that `E-WHOLE-WORK-IS-ONE-TILE-1` flagged as stopping the faithful DeepNSM run.

**The strange-noun problem.** A KG is ABOUT its named entities (Nebuchadnezzar, Melchizedek, Israel), but those proper nouns + archaic content words are OUT-OF-VOCAB in a FIXED global vocabulary (DeepNSM's 4096 COCA). Measured: a fixed 4096-COCA vocab **DROPS 2,888 / 3,519 = 82% of the Bible's proper nouns** — it drops exactly the entities the graph exists to represent. That is why "count the whole Bible's entities with DeepNSM" ceilings at ~4096 and is vacuous.

**The fix (operator): the corpus's OWN frequency-ranked lemma list IS the codebook.** The Bible's 12,555 distinct types, ranked by frequency (`E-FREQ-IS-COSINE-REPLACEMENT-1` — the rank IS the address, computed in one counting pass), ARE the `u16` address space. Every token is in it BY CONSTRUCTION → **zero OOV**. Measured ranks: frequent names in the head (`lord` 14, `god` 27, `israel` 49, `david` 106, `jesus` 114, `moses` 133); rare names in the tail but still addressed (`nebuchadnezzar` 1053, `melchizedek` 6950, `mahershalalhashbaz` — the Bible's longest name, freq 2 — rank 8034). All 3,519 proper nouns ≤ rank 12,539 < 64k, median rank 7,725. The 12k list drops **0**.

**Design consequence: the codebook is PER-WORK, not global.** One work = one 64k tile = its own frequency-ranked lemma codebook (≤64k, OOV-free). This is the OGAR canon's *"codebook scoping = the class routing prefix"* applied at the lemma layer — each work/class gets its own `256⁶` centroid space, and the frequency axis fills its addresses for FREE (one count pass); only the DISTANCE metric (the 256-centroid ADC codebook) needs training. Half the codebook is free per work; the strange nouns are handled by construction, not by a bigger global vocab.

**Unblocks the faithful whole-work run.** With the per-work 12k lemma codebook, the DeepNSM-style entity count is no longer vocab-ceiling'd — build the freq-ranked lemma list from the corpus, map every token (incl. strange nouns) to its rank, extract SPO, count nodes. (The `protoc` blocker on `text_stream_to_soa` remains an environment issue; the vocab-resolution blocker is DISSOLVED.) **Cross-ref:** `E-WHOLE-WORK-IS-ONE-TILE-1` (one work = one tile, the measured lexical bound), `E-FREQ-IS-COSINE-REPLACEMENT-1` (freq rank = the free address), `E-NAMING-HEURISTIC-CAPITALIZATION-1` (proper-noun detection by capitalization), OGAR `INTEGRATION-MAP` codebook-scoping-per-class canon, `two-basin-routing.md`.

## 2026-07-21 — E-WHOLE-WORK-IS-ONE-TILE-1 — operator ruling: even the Bible or Lord of the Rings has ≤ 64k SPO, and **64k = 256:256** — so causality's "escalate to absolute" target is NOT unbounded, it is ONE 256×256 centroid tile that holds the ENTIRE work's SPO graph (~16 MB resident, u16-addressable). This BOUNDS `E-GRAMMAR-LOCAL-CAUSAL-ABSOLUTE-1` and UNIFIES its two targets: `part_of:is_a` and centroid are the TWO JOBS of the SAME tile (routing vs metric), not two stores

**Status:** FINDING (operator ruling, grounded in two shipped board findings). **Confidence:** High — the 64k bound is measured/validated, and `64k = 256:256 = one CAM-PQ tile` is already the substrate's canonical structure. Collapses the escalation ladder from "unbounded search over time" to "one bounded tile."

**The bound is measured, not asserted — with a concrete whole-work anchor.** `AGENT_LOG` (:981/:983): Animal Farm + 1984 → **9.2k SPO / 4.71 MB**; the whole-book capacity claim ≈ **32k SPO nodes / ~16 MB** resident. The operator's anchor makes it concrete: **the entire Bible = 31,102 verses (66 books, 1,189 chapters)** — a verse ≈ one SPO-ish unit, so the whole Bible ≈ **31k units ≈ half a 64k tile**, landing almost exactly on the board's 32k whole-book estimate. And the granularity is comfortable, not strained: the Bible is **~785,000 English words**, so a full 64k-SPO budget is `785,000 / 64,000 ≈` **12 words per SPO** — exactly clause / proposition granularity (an S-P-O proposition is ~10–15 words). So one 256×256 tile holds the entire Bible not merely verse-coarse (31k verses, ~25 words each, ~half the tile) but at the **natural fine proposition grain**, still inside one tile. Bible / LOTR ≤ 64k SPO is therefore grounded, not hand-waved (heavy coreference collapse keeps the NODE count — the u16-addressable entities/concepts, not raw triple count — ≤ 64k even for the largest narratives). **So an entire work's causal-SPO graph fits in a `u16` address space, with headroom.**

**64k = 256:256 = ONE tile (the operator's punchline).** `65536 = 256 × 256` = exactly one CAM-PQ centroid tile = one HEEL/HIP/TWIG tier of the canon key. `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1` already ruled this tile IMPLICIT: never materialize the 64k pairwise table — store the 256 centroids (2 KB texture), regenerate the distance shader on demand. So the whole-work SPO graph IS one implicit 256×256 tile.

**Consequence 1 — the escalation is BOUNDED, not unbounded.** `E-GRAMMAR-LOCAL-CAUSAL-ABSOLUTE-1` said causality past the ±8 horizon "escalates to an absolute address." This bounds WHAT that address space is: a **single 256×256 tile** holding the whole work. A causal antecedent (the feud) is one `u16` in that tile. Romeo & Juliet's death (a `u16` node) → `Kausal` → the feud (a `u16` node) = **one edge in a whole-work-in-one-tile graph**, an O(1) lookup into a ~16 MB resident structure — never a search over unbounded time.

**Consequence 2 — the two escalation targets UNIFY into one tile's two jobs.** `E-GRAMMAR-LOCAL-CAUSAL-ABSOLUTE-1` named "`part_of:is_a` OR centroid" as the absolute target. They are NOT two stores — they are the TWO JOBS of the SAME 256×256 tile (`E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1`): **routing** (address-only shift/mask over the 4⁴ centroid hierarchy, ρ≈0.51 coarse) = `part_of:is_a` basin membership; **metric** (ADC over the stored 256 centroids, ρ=1.0) = centroid semantic similarity. The escalation resolver reads ONE tile TWO ways, not two structures.

**Consequence 3 — "minimal resistance = non-limit" made fully concrete (`E-HORIZON-NOT-BOUND-1`).** The whole work is only 64k, so it is ALWAYS fully addressable; reaching any causal antecedent is a `u16` lookup into a resident tile. "Unbounded awareness" precisely means "the entire work fits in one tile," not "search infinity." The ±8/16 is the cheap local read; the escalation home is one 64k tile — both bounded, both cheap.

**MEASURED this session on the real KJV text (the falsifier, not an extrapolation).** Fetched the public-domain KJV (Project Gutenberg #10), stripped to the text body (**792,180 word tokens** — matches the ~785k estimate), and counted lexical cardinality directly: **12,555 distinct word types** (the whole vocabulary = **0.19 of a 64k tile**, one tile holds it **5.2× over**); **3,523 proper-noun types** (names/places — the S/O *entity* nodes = **0.05 of a tile**, ~1/18); and at the measured ~12-words-per-SPO proposition grain, `792k / 12 ≈` **66k propositions ≈ exactly one tile**. Since a graph NODE is an entity/lemma, the Bible's distinct-node count is bounded above by **12,555 ≪ 64k**; at fact/proposition grain it lands at ~one tile. So the whole-work-in-one-tile bound is **confirmed on real text**. Footprint: **64k × 512 B/NodeRow = 32 MB** — the entire Bible's SoA knowledge graph is a 32 MB resident block (the ~16 MB board figure was the ~32k-node half-tile; the full tile is 32 MB). *Honest boundary:* the full DeepNSM "test the awareness" run (standing-wave escalation rate, deductions/contradictions over the real stream) was NOT run here — `text_stream_to_soa` needs `protoc` (absent in this env) and DeepNSM's 4096-COCA vocab is the wrong resolution to count whole-Bible entities (it ceilings at ~4096). The lexical bound above is the real, tractable falsifier; the awareness run is deferred to a protoc + full-vocab environment.

**The frequency axis is FREE (measured, ties to `E-FREQ-IS-COSINE-REPLACEMENT-1`).** The operator's point: `vocabulary = frequency × distance`, and frequency is trivially computable (one counting pass). Measured on the KJV: **Zipf holds** (`freq × rank` ≈ flat: 64k→90k→113k→63k across ranks 1–1000); the top word `the` = **64,113 ≈ one whole tile**; and the EFFECTIVE vocabulary is tiny — **43 types cover 50%** of the running text, **1,149 cover 90%**, 2,406 cover 95%, 6,714 cover 99%. So the 12,555 types are frequency-ranked ADDRESSES filling 19% of one 64k tile, and the semantically-active band (content words, ~1k–2.4k types for 90–95% coverage) is under 4% of the tile. `frequency × distance` = the tile's two jobs (`E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1`): **frequency → the address** (Zipf rank = the gridlake/centroid position, computed by counting), **distance → the metric** (the CAM-PQ 256-centroid codebook, ADC). Half the vocabulary's identity is free; only the distance codebook needs training.

**Consequence 4 — the tile is the UNIT, not the ceiling; scale-beyond-64k is the CASCADE, never field-widening.** One 256×256 tile handles ANY codebook up to 64k — the 4096 COCA vocabulary and a 20k academic vocabulary both fit one tile with headroom (20k, 4096 ≪ 64k). Past 64k the substrate does NOT widen the field — it adds the next cascade LEVEL: `HEEL→HIP→TWIG` are three 256×256 tiers (the CAM-PQ `6×256` path), giving `256⁶ ≈ 2.8×10¹⁴` cells per class, each tier's codebook selected by the routing prefix (OGAR canon: *"codebook scoping = the class routing prefix"*; *"capacity through hierarchy, never widening"*; *"scale = the next cascade level, never field-widening"*). So "past the 64k horizon" is the SAME SHAPE one level up — the tile composes, the `u16` stays a `u16`. Whole-work-in-one-tile (Consequence 1) and cascade-for-more (this) are the one rule at two scales.

**Code / D-CSW-2 implication.** The absolute resolver is a lookup into ONE implicit 256×256 centroid tile (the whole-work SPO), doing routing (`part_of:is_a` shift/mask) then metric (ADC over 256 centroids) — NOT a multi-store escalation cascade; and if a corpus exceeds 64k, one more cascade tier, not a wider address. Bounded, `u16` per tier, ~16 MB resident, the 2 KB centroid texture + on-demand shader (never the 64k table). This is the D-CSW-2 design, now sized. **Cross-ref:** `E-GRAMMAR-LOCAL-CAUSAL-ABSOLUTE-1` (this bounds + unifies its targets), `E-IMPLICIT-MORTON-TILE-1` (the 256:256=64k implicit tile, routing-vs-metric two jobs), `E-HORIZON-NOT-BOUND-1` (minimal resistance / non-limit), `AGENT_LOG` :981/:983 (measured 9.2k / whole-book ≈ 32k SPO), the canon key (HEEL/HIP/TWIG = 256×256 centroid tiles), D-CSW-2 (the resolver this sizes).

## 2026-07-21 — E-GRAMMAR-LOCAL-CAUSAL-ABSOLUTE-1 — operator ruling: the ±8 reference horizon is DIMENSIONED FOR GRAMMAR (local dependencies resolve as positional i4 offsets, and settle there); CAUSALITY routinely lives PAST ±8 and escalates by a REPRESENTATION SWITCH to an ABSOLUTE address — `part_of:is_a` (AriGraph episodic basin) OR centroid (CAM-PQ / palette256) — never a wider offset. This is why the two are distinct and neither is lifted into the other

**Status:** FINDING (operator architectural ruling — *"grammar needs ±8 buffer; causality might be past the ±8 and need a part_of:is_a or centroid"*). **Confidence:** High — grounded in the A9 loci semantics (which already carry both kinds) + the shipped two-basin routing. Sharpens `E-HORIZON-NOT-BOUND-1` (which established escalation ≠ coincidental) by naming WHAT escalation resolves TO, and answers the causality-vs-episodic question (prior turn).

**Two kinds of relationship, two representations — the ±8 buffer belongs to ONLY ONE of them:**

- **GRAMMAR is LOCAL and POSITIONAL.** Relative-pronoun→antecedent, verb→SPO arguments, TEKAMOLO role-filling, modifier attachment — grammatical dependencies resolve within ~8 tokens. The i4 offset (`∈−8..+7`) IS the complete answer; **the ±8 window is dimensioned exactly for grammatical dependency length.** The A9 grammar loci — `Antecedent`, `SMeaning`/`PMeaning`/`OMeaning`, `Modal`, `Lokal`, `MeaningLevel`, `Temporal` — SETTLE in the reference horizon (`WaveGrounding::Causal`); that is their native home. A grammar locus leaving ±8 is anomalous (a genuinely long-distance dependency, rare), not a routine escalation.

- **CAUSALITY is OFTEN NON-LOCAL and ABSOLUTE.** A cause and its effect can be arbitrarily far apart (Romeo & Juliet's death ← the feud, hundreds of tokens back). The i4 offset is only the cheap LOCAL GUESS; when the causal chain leaves the reference horizon it does **not widen the offset** — it **SWITCHES REPRESENTATION to an absolute address**:
  - **`part_of:is_a`** — AriGraph episodic basin membership (the `BasinAnchor`/`SupportedBy`/`Supports` rails, EW64): "same episode / same theme" — the feud basin.
  - **centroid** — CAM-PQ / palette256 hierarchical centroid (the 256×256 tile, the OGAR path bytes): "same semantic region" — an absolute semantic address, not a position.

  The A9 causal/basin loci — `Kausal`, `BasinAnchor` (already `part_of:is_a`!), `SupportedBy`, `Supports` — are the ones for which escalation-to-absolute is NORMAL, not anomalous.

**The seam is a representation switch, not a window-widen (`I-VSA-IDENTITIES` + two-basin routing).** Past the reference horizon you address by IDENTITY (basin / centroid), not by OFFSET — exactly the register-not-content and positional-vs-semantic doctrines, applied at the escalation boundary. This is precisely why `resolve_chain` does NOT widen the i4 (it sets `escalated=true` and hands off): a wider offset would be the wrong TOOL for causality, which past ±8 is no longer positional at all.

**This ANSWERS causality-vs-episodic (prior turn).** Episodic is NOT lifted into causal, and causal is not lifted into episodic. Causality is the DIRECTED ARROW (`Kausal`, escalates); episodic (`part_of:is_a` basin) is ONE of the two ABSOLUTE HOMES causality LANDS IN when it's non-local (centroid is the other). You need episodic edges precisely because they are a causal-escalation target — the absolute basin the arrow reaches into. The A9 register unifies the POINTERS (grammar loci + causal loci in one 24-slot register); the underlying EDGES stay distinct (grammar = positional i4; causal-past-horizon = `part_of:is_a`/centroid absolute).

**Code status.** `standing_wave_grounded`/`GateOutcome::Escalate` already emit the correct signal with NO i4 widening (`E-HORIZON-NOT-BOUND-1`); the ABSOLUTE resolver (route an escalated `Kausal` chain to `part_of:is_a` basin OR centroid) is **D-CSW-2, unbuilt**. A `Locus` grammar-vs-causal classification would let that resolver route per-locus — grammar loci: settle-or-anomaly; causal loci: escalate-to-absolute — **proposed, not built** (awaiting operator go). **Cross-ref:** `E-HORIZON-NOT-BOUND-1` (escalation ≠ coincidental — this names its target), `two-basin-routing.md` (positional vs semantic), `I-VSA-IDENTITIES` (address by identity past the horizon), OGAR tier-interpretation (the 256×256 centroid tile), D-CSW-2 (the absolute resolver — the build this gates), `causal_witness::Locus` (the loci that carry both concerns).

## 2026-07-21 — E-HORIZON-NOT-BOUND-1 — operator ruling: the ±8 nibble is only the REFERENCE HORIZON, never a bound on awareness — a causal chain that leaves it is NOT coincidental (Romeo & Juliet's death is still caused by the distant feud), it ESCALATES to search causality over time. CORRECTS the `WaveGrounding::Coincidental` / `GateOutcome::WaveCatch` labels shipped hours earlier → renamed `Escalate`. Escalation resolves relative→ABSOLUTE: temporal.rs version-range → AriGraph SPO tropes → Leiden families, none limited to the 16-locus window; the horizon's resistance is minimal (16×3 SPO + 16×3 AriGraph ≈ a non-limit)

**Status:** SHIPPED-CORRECTION (code + doctrine) + FINDING (operator architectural ruling). **Confidence:** High — the mislabel was a real code defect (`standing_wave_grounded` collapsed "chain left ±8" into `Coincidental`); the fix is verified (contract green, clippy clean, `dispatch_guard_redundancy` 34/34 `Fires→Escalate`). CORRECTS the label in `E-MARKOV-STANDING-WAVE-GATE-1` and `E-SUDOKU-TISSUE-WEAVE-1` (append-only correction; those entries' "coincidental/WaveCatch" reading is superseded by "Escalate" here).
