Over four reasoning states of increasing engagement — with `confidence` (0.50→0.75) and `contradiction` MEASURED from live `TripletGraph`s (`revise_with_evidence` / `detect_contradictions`), so the immersion is earned not asserted — felt-intensity (L1 norm of the qualia lanes) rose **7 → 8 → 16 → 38** (flat fact → re-read → surprise → held contradiction; `wonder`=√(coherence·expansion) 0→5.00). **PASS: monotone ✓.** The self-read closed the loop: the system generated `arousal+5 valence-5 tension+7 depth+7 entropy+4 coherence+5 expansion+5` while reasoning the held contradiction, then read it back as *"held taut between two truths, grave with necessity, startled, tragic, clear and settled"* — the first-person report IS the subject reading its own felt state. KILL would have been flat qualia regardless of immersion (the identity is decoration); it did not fire. The exhibit demonstrates the architecture; it does not (and the boundary above says it cannot) prove phenomenal consciousness.
## 2026-07-19 — E-CAUSAL-KNOWLEDGE-TRANSFER-1 — a fable's MEANING is its causality trajectory; understanding one story transfers to a separate story (moral + wired qualia) by signature match, no re-derivation — the reason-about-itself + knowledge-transfer endgame piece, no LLM

**Status:** FINDING (built + measured). **Confidence:** High. Deliverable: `crates/lance-graph/examples/causal_knowledge_transfer.rs`. Operator: *"understand the meaning of Scorpion and frog and do knowledge transfer to other people in a separate story … also please wire qualia … Kanbanstep driven strategies + W-D/W-B > causality trajectories."* Makes `E-REASONING-CORE-GROUND-TRUTH-1`'s cross-text-transfer claim runnable.

A **causality trajectory** is the relation signature `agent --has--> NATURE --compels--> ACT --causes--> HARM`, Pearl-rung-decomposed: SO=association, PO=`do(¬ACT)`→no-harm=**avoidable**, SPO=`NATURE ⊨ ACT`⇒do(¬ACT) unreachable=**necessary**; the moral = avoidable ∧ necessary (a preserved contradiction, not a scalar). The fable's MEANING is a property of that SIGNATURE, not the entities — so understanding transfers to any story sharing it.

**Qualia WIRED (`QualiaI4_16D`, the felt magnitude):** the preserved-contradiction moral carries `tension=+7` (both poles held), `depth=+6` (modal necessity), `valence=-5` (tragic), `coherence=+4`, `expansion=+4`, and derived `wonder(Staunen)=√(coherence·expansion)=4.00`. The felt tragedy is part of the meaning and transfers with it.

**Measured:**
- **Story A (scorpion & frog)** — understood: avoidable ✓ ∧ necessary ✓; qualia as above. Trajectory signature learned.
- **Story B (separate people: `marcus --has--> ambition --compels--> betrays --causes--> ruin`, victim Elena)** — signature MATCHES → meaning **INHERITED** (no counterfactual re-run): Marcus's betrayal is avoidable ∧ necessary = the SAME moral, with the SAME qualia (`wonder=4.00`). Knowledge transferred.
- **Story C (`merchant --sells--> cloth`, no `compels`)** — signature ABSENT → no trajectory → the moral does NOT transfer. The control proves transfer is STRUCTURAL, not universal.

**Composition (the operator's "Kanbanstep + W-D/W-B > causality trajectories"):** W-B (the 2³ rung fan) GENERATES the trajectory's rungs; W-D (the 34 tactic recipes) does the analogy/transfer; the kanban step-strategies DRIVE the cycle; `QualiaI4_16D` CARRIES the felt magnitude. Here the trajectory + transfer are composed directly over the shipped `TripletGraph` (`intervene_on` for Rung 2, modal-chain check for Rung 3) + `QualiaI4_16D` — the honest boundary: production generates/dispatches the trajectory via the (still-unwired) W-B/W-D/kanban machinery; this proves the transfer + qualia mechanism end-to-end. Additive: example only, no core change.
## 2026-07-19 — E-X265-MORTON-SHIFT-1b — third Codex P2 (on PR #746, landed as follow-up): `morton_translate_checked` must guard `delta ≥ AXIS` before dilating

**Status:** FINDING (2 tests extended, 8 green; follow-up to `E-X265-MORTON-SHIFT-1a`, again landed as a new PR because #746 merged before the review posted). Real: `dilate8` masks to the low 8 bits, so a delta `≥ 256` (e.g. `dx=256 → dilate8(256)=dilate8(0)=0`) spoofs "no motion" and returns `Some` at the wrapped address while the ground-truth raster clips every pixel out. Fix: reject `dx >= AXIS || dy >= AXIS` up front (such a delta moves the whole sprite out of a 256-field anyway). Also corrected the stale "bit 17 for y" doc-comment (the y carry stops at bit 16). **Meta-lesson:** three Codex P2s, all on the SAME edge-case axis (field-boundary behavior of the toroidal add) — the probe's happy-path tests never crossed the edge; the fix wasn't complete until the boundary was tested at EVERY delta magnitude (interior, edge-crossing, ≥AXIS). **Cross-ref:** `E-X265-MORTON-SHIFT-1a`, `-1`; Codex P2 on PR #746.

## 2026-07-19 — E-X265-MORTON-SHIFT-1a — post-merge hardening (Codex P2×2 on PR #745, landed as follow-up): the dilated-lane add is TOROIDAL, and dis-occlusion must clamp

**Status:** FINDING (follow-up to `E-X265-MORTON-SHIFT-1`; PR #745 merged before the two Codex P2s could land, so they ship as a new PR per the merged-PR rule; 8 tests green). Both P2s were real:
1. **Toroidal wrap at field edges.** The Morton dilated-lane add wraps mod 256 per lane, so a translate carrying a pixel past the field edge produced a *wrapped* small coordinate that passed the bounds check and wrote a garbage pixel — while the ground-truth raster *clips*. Hand-picked interior deltas never triggered it, but the mechanism claim needed it: added `morton_translate_checked` (detects lane overflow directly — the carry out of x's top bit lands in bit 16, y's likewise ripples to bit 16 and stops since XMASK doesn't cover it), and the address render now clips to match GT. So bit-exactness holds at the EDGE, not only the interior. **The toroidal property is a real feature, not a bug** — a shift on the torus — now stated explicitly and tested (`edge_crossing_translate_is_clipped_bit_exact`, `checked_translate_detects_field_exit`).
2. **Dis-occlusion inclusion-exclusion underflowed.** `dx·h + dy·w − dx·dy` over-subtracts once `dx≥w`/`dy≥h` and underflows (u32) for large deltas (e.g. `dx=dy=100` on a 24² sprite). Fixed by clamping `dx,dy` to the sprite extents first → caps at `w·h` (the whole sprite is new when there's no overlap).
**Lesson (process):** gating caught MY own off-by-one first (I initially checked y-overflow at bit 17; the carry actually stops at bit 16) — the probe discipline (assert + run before claiming) held on the fix itself. **Cross-ref:** `E-X265-MORTON-SHIFT-1` (parent); Codex P2s on PR #745.

## 2026-07-19 — E-MULTIHOP-WITNESS-CONFIDENCE-1 — Truth from the multi-hop witness chain (V3 substrate): CausalEdge64's u8 NARS truth + the WitnessTable W-slot arc, richer than single-fact revision — the operator's "Truth > multi-hop witness confidence"

**Status:** FINDING (built + measured). **Confidence:** High. **Walk CORRECTED post-merge per Codex #748 `r3610142483`:** the first cut conflated the fact HANDLE with the W-slot (synthetic IDs == slots); the walk now stores each fact's own slot in its edge (`with_w_slot`, real under the default v2 layout) and follows `w_slot()` at every hop, using `spo_fact_ref` strictly as an opaque fact handle (slot != handle via a permutation) — identical `0.502→0.858` result, now a valid template for the real V3 witness walk. Deliverable: `crates/lance-graph/examples/multihop_witness_confidence.rs`. Operator direction: *"episodic basins get supporting edges from witness entries and implement both anchors and nars Truth/frequency value already present in causaledge64 … Truth > multi-hop witness confidence … richer expressiveness in v3."*

`E-SELF-CORRECTING-KG-1` (W-C in-process) used the string `TripletGraph` + f32 `TruthValue` — the SINGLE-hop revision (re-observe the same fact → confidence up). The **V3-rich** form composes the shipped V3 primitives: facts are `CausalEdge64` carrying the u8 NARS truth **already present** in the edge (`frequency_u8`/`confidence_u8`); the `lance_graph_contract::witness_table::WitnessTable<64>` holds the **W-slot arc** — each `WitnessEntry.spo_fact_ref` is the hop back to the prior belief (the "Markov-style belief-update arc" the witness-table doc names). A fact's Truth is NARS-revised across its **whole multi-hop witness chain**, not just its own observation.

**Measured (one episodic basin — shared predicate:object = the `part_of:is_a` rail; each fact a single 0.502-confidence observation):**

| fact | single-obs conf | multi-hop conf | witness hops |
|---|---|---|---|
| F0 | 0.502 | 0.502 | 1 |
| F1 | 0.502 | 0.668 | 2 |
| F2 | 0.502 | 0.751 | 3 |
| F3 | 0.502 | 0.801 | 4 |
| F4 | 0.502 | 0.834 | 5 |
| F5 | 0.502 | **0.858** | 6 |

Single-observation confidence is flat; **multi-hop confidence rises monotonically with witness depth** — a belief supported by a deeper chain of independent witnesses is more certain. The three operator pieces map exactly: **anchor** = shared `p:o` (`part_of:is_a` basin); **supporting edges** = the witness entries (the W-slot arc); **Truth** = the u8 NARS value in `CausalEdge64`, revised over the arc. This is the richer v3 expressiveness over `self_correcting_kg`'s single-fact revision — the same NARS math, now driven by the episodic witness chain.

**Scope / honest boundary:** composes the SHIPPED primitives (`CausalEdge64` accessors + `WitnessTable` + f32 NARS revision reading the edge's u8 truth) in an example — **no contract-layout change** (so no `v3-envelope-auditor` / `v3-mailbox-warden` gate). The arc is keyed by fact index (feature-agnostic); the production **edge→W-slot bit-storage** (`with_w_slot`, bits 53–58) wired into the `MailboxSoA` emission path is the gated V3 slice (W-C.2 / the witness-emission wave, per `witness_table.rs` "later slices" + the trajectory map). The revision uses the f32 formula on the u8 truth; the tabled `NarsTables::revise` (u8→u8) is the hot-path swap-in.
## 2026-07-19 — E-SELF-CORRECTING-KG-1 — the knowledge graph stops starting from zero: re-reading REVISES in place and NARS confidence accumulates (W-C in-process, the endgame's "self-correcting" core)

**Status:** FINDING (built + measured). **Confidence:** High. Deliverable: `crates/lance-graph/examples/self_correcting_kg.rs`. Operator endgame: *"a self-correcting knowledge graph from reading without LLM that is able to reason about itself."* Operator complaint it answers: *"it can't be that every pass starts from Zero."*

The shipped S07 pipeline (`text_stream_to_soa`) does `TripletGraph::new()` every run — it recomputes 95,410 deductions from zero and discards them (E-S07 / trajectory-cartographer map). W-C flips that with the ALREADY-SHIPPED `TripletGraph::revise_with_evidence` (`triplet_graph.rs:904`): a re-observed `(S,R,O)` is found and its `TruthValue.revision` accumulates confidence (`w/(w+k)`) instead of being rebuilt. The ONLY difference between the two runs is a single `new()`.

**Measured (each text mention seeded as 0.5-confidence evidence, so revision is visible — `certain()`=1.0 is already saturated):**
- **Aesop** — naive (`new()` per pass): 10 triplets, witness `"lion catch mouse"` confidence **0.500 every pass** (from zero, no memory). Self-correcting (one graph, 3 re-readings): pass 1 → 10 triplets @ 0.500; pass 2 → **0 new** @ **0.667**; pass 3 → **0 new** @ **0.750**.
- **Animal Farm** — naive: 4012 triplets from zero each pass, static 0.500. Self-correcting: 4012 pass 1, **0 new** pass 2/3, confidence **0.500 → 0.667 → 0.750**; 95,410 deductions + 20,131 contradictions on the ACCUMULATED graph (contradictions retained, not collapsed — the "reason about itself" seed, per E-REASONING-CORE-GROUND-TRUTH-1 pt 4).

**The point:** the naive graph rebuilds N triplets from zero every pass and its confidence never moves; the self-correcting graph adds 0 after pass 1, revises in place, and its confidence rises — **re-reading the same text makes it *surer*, not busier.** This is the endgame's self-correction mechanism running with zero LLM.

**Scope / honest boundary:** this proves the **revise-not-recompute** logic IN-PROCESS (across passes in one run). **Durable cross-process persistence** — materialize `Triplet`→`NodeRow` (Meta tenant `nars_f`/`nars_c`) via `NodeRowPacket`→Lance + hydrate-on-entry, so the graph survives a restart — is **W-C.2** (the byte-locked tenant lanes all exist per the trajectory map; only the write-back + read-on-entry is missing). Dead-end counterfactual memory (`ContextTag::Contradiction` + −6 mantissa read-on-entry) is W-E. Additive: new example only; no core change. All lance-graph tests unaffected.
## 2026-07-19 — E-X265-MORTON-SHIFT-1 — the non-tautological head-to-head-2: rigid translation of an ARBITRARY sprite IS a Morton address-delta (dilated-lane add), bit-exact + O(1) in sprite size — the "moving = bit-shifting to adjacent" claim CONFIRMED structurally; x265 categorically lacks the substrate

**Status:** FINDING (leg-1 probe RAN, deterministic, 6 tests green; the real-x265 quantitative sub-pel-appearance leg is v2). Operator claim (this session): *"x265 never had blasgraph + neighborhood using Morton-cascade inverse-pyramid 4×4 ergonomics with the Pythagorean comma (akin to the Fujifilm X-Trans sensor), and moving a sprite could become bit-shifting to adjacent."* This probe makes the last clause falsifiable and confirms it for rigid tile-aligned motion.
**Deliverable:** `crates/helix/examples/morton_shift_motion_probe.rs` (std-only, SplitMix64 no-`rand`, `MSHIFT …` greppable, 6 tests).
**Why it is NOT `E-X265-HEADTOHEAD-1`'s tautology:** there the sprite APPEARANCE was φ-generated (model-matched) → ∞-PSNR was structural. Here the appearance is **arbitrary deterministic-noise, never φ-placed**, and is paid for EQUALLY on both sides — ONLY the motion representation is compared. So the advantage is about how motion is coded, not a matched appearance model. This is the stronger, honest result.
**The run (256×256 field, 24×24 sprite):**
```
legA dx∈{(1,0),(0,1),(7,5),(16,16)}  bit_exact=1  motion_bytes=2  sprite_px=576   ← one (dx,dy) moves all 576 px, bit-exact
legA_dilated_add_identity=1   ← morton2(x+dx,y+dy) == two carry-through-holes adds (4096 random samples)
legB disocc_dx=2 dy=1 disocc_px=70 disocc_frac=0.1215   ← interior residual = 0; only the revealed L-strip is new content
legC comma_three_gap_distinct=3  comma_coprime_full_perm=1   ← sub-pel dither aperiodic (Fujifilm X-Trans move)
```
**The mechanism (three sound points, grounded):**
1. **Rigid translation = dilated-lane integer add (carry-through-the-holes).** `morton2(x+dx, y+dy)` equals `morton_add_y(morton_add_x(m, dx), dy)` — proven over 4096 samples + a 20k-sample test. The SAME `(dx,dy)` delta applied once moves EVERY sprite pixel identically → the whole object translates for the cost of one delta (2 bytes here), **independent of the 576 pixels**. x265 has no address to add to; it codes an MV field on a raster (merge/AMVP recover the correlation, they don't ARE it).
2. **Bit-exact interior, dis-occlusion is the only tail.** Address-delta reconstruction matches the re-anchored ground truth byte-for-byte (interior residual = 0 bytes, where x265's DCT+quant round-trip is never exactly 0). The genuine new content is the revealed leading-edge L-strip (`dx·h + dy·w − dx·dy` px) — measured, not hidden. It is exactly the residual the entropy coder (E-3DGS-MU-HYDRATION cross-ref: the tail that needs a real coder) must still carry. **Shrinks the tail to genuine-new-information; does not erase it.**
3. **Sub-pel = the comma phase, not a quarter-pel filter (the Fujifilm X-Trans move).** Sub-tile placement rides the deterministic aperiodic-by-coprimality phase (`E-COMMA-PERTURBATION-PHASE-1`); three-gap=3 + coprime-perm confirm it can't alias against the tile grid — 0 stored bits, no 8-tap interpolation filter. (User said "Fujitsu"; the reference is Fujifilm's X-Trans 6×6 aperiodic CFA that drops the OLPF by incommensurability — the §7 comma-closure anchor.)
**Verdict: PASS→[H] for the STRUCTURAL claim** — rigid tile-aligned translation of an arbitrary sprite is O(1) address arithmetic, bit-exact, and the substrate (Morton address + blasgraph shift operator over the HHTL neighborhood) is one x265 categorically never had. This is the concrete **"MortonShift" prediction MODE** for the add-a-mode/RDO integration (my parity answer this session): RDO picks it where motion is rigid+addressable, falls back to block-ME otherwise → ≥ parity by construction, win on the addressable regions.
**Honest fences (v2 gates):** (a) the advantage scales INVERSELY with per-frame motion magnitude — dis-occlusion grows with the delta (24 px at (1,0) → 512 px at (16,16)); strongest for small motion (high-frame-rate common case). (b) Non-rigid motion (rotation/scale/deform/occlusion-reveal) is NOT a bit-shift — out of scope by construction. (c) Sub-pel **appearance** (not just position) is the inverse-pyramid-descent question (descend one cascade level to the finer 4×4 tile) — the real-content v2 leg, NOT proven here. (d) Cross-cascade-level carry (a large object translating at a coarse level) is bounded but not free — v2. (e) Real-x265 inter-frame bytes at matched PSNR is the deferred quantitative leg (like mu-hydration's real-`.ply`).
**Cross-ref:** `E-X265-HEADTOHEAD-1` (head-to-head-1, the appearance-model-matched lower bound this removes the confound from); `E-COMMA-PERTURBATION-PHASE-1` (φ/coprime comma = the aperiodic sub-pel dither); `E-3DGS-MU-HYDRATION-2` (the addressing-dependence sibling — motion HAS the index a static cloud lacks); ndarray plan `x265-sprite-replay-probe-v1.md`; parity write-up (add-a-mode + RDO, this session's chat).

## 2026-07-19 — E-3DGS-MU-HYDRATION-2 — PROBE-SPLAT-μ-HYDRATION-RHO leg 1 RAN: static spatially-addressed μ-hydration KILLED (escalation ≈ 1.0), but the mechanism is sharper than a plain DROP — hydration is ADDRESSING-dependent (motion-only), and the comma is exactly what decouples generation-index from spatial-address

**Status:** FINDING (leg-1 probe RAN, deterministic, 5 tests green; leg 1 = synthetic-structured falsifier per the parent entry's probe spec — the real-`.ply` `splat3d::ply` leg stays the named v2). Adjudicates `E-3DGS-MU-HYDRATION-1`'s `PROBE-SPLAT-μ-HYDRATION-RHO` sub-claim (1) (address-derived μ); the parent's KILL condition was "escalation ≈ 100% → positions-free false → collapses to the ordinary SoA splat3d already ships" — **that condition is MET**, so the static-cloud seam DROPS. The `ctrl_naddr` leg turns the plain DROP into a mechanism.
**Deliverable:** `crates/helix/examples/mu_hydration_probe.rs` (std-only, `helix::placement` public API, SplitMix64 no-`rand`, `MUHYD …` greppable lines).
**The run (N=4096, GRID=64):**
```
set=ctrl_naddr  mean_res=0.0000 rho=1.0000 escalation=0.0000   ← φ-phase at GENERATION index: PERFECT hydration
set=ctrl        mean_res=0.4904 rho=-0.0198 escalation=0.9978  ← SAME cloud, MORTON (spatial) address: collapses
set=nonphi      mean_res=0.6592 rho=-0.0676 escalation=1.0000  ← structured-synthetic cloud, spatial address: no win
comma_phi_three_gap_distinct=3   ← Steinhaus ≤3 (golden rotation aperiodic — anti-moiré certificate)
comma_coprime_full_perm=1        ← stride-4-over-17 full permutation (discrete comma)
```
**The mechanism (the `ctrl_naddr` contrast is load-bearing):** the SAME control cloud hydrates `rho=1.0` (residual 0) at its **generation-index** address and `rho≈0` (escalation ≈100%) at its **Morton spatial** address. So φ-phase hydration is NOT a property of the data — it is a property of the **address**. A static gaussian cloud's only intrinsic address is spatial (Morton over quantized μ); it has **no sequential generation index**. The space-filling φ-spiral — literally the **comma** (`E-COMMA-PERTURBATION-PHASE-1`: φ incommensurate, never closes) — scatters the generation index `n` across the grid, so a spatial address provably **cannot** recover `n`, and the φ-phase device has no index to ride. **μ-hydration ⊂ motion-hydration:** motion HAS the index (path/frame counter — exactly the `E-X265-HEADTOHEAD-1` ∞-PSNR case, which works BECAUSE the frame index is transmitted), a static cloud does not.
**Verdict: DROP for static spatially-addressed μ-hydration** (it collapses to the ordinary float-SoA `splat3d` already ships — no positions-free win). **The φ-hydration seam SURVIVES only where a sequential generation index exists and is transmitted** (motion trajectories, procedurally-generated-in-index clouds) — NOT arbitrary trained Inria `.ply` geometry. The parent's "WORTH-EXPLORING on the φ-manifold" is thus refined: the manifold is not a geometric sub-region of μ-space, it is the presence-of-a-generation-index — which static reconstruction lacks by construction.
**Comma fence (orthogonal, both legs green):** three-gap-distinct = 3 confirms `{n·φ}` is aperiodic (Steinhaus three-gap theorem, the CORRECT low-discrepancy certificate — replaced a mis-specified Pearson lag-autocorrelation of the rotation, which measures the near-constant golden STEP, not periodicity); coprime full-perm = 1 confirms the discrete stride-4-over-17 comma. The comma is not just the anti-moiré enabler (`E-COMMA-PERTURBATION-PHASE-1` §4) — it is ALSO the exact reason the static seam drops: incommensurability is what makes the spatial address unable to invert to `n`.
**Cross-ref:** `E-3DGS-MU-HYDRATION-1` (parent; KILL condition met for leg 1); `E-COMMA-PERTURBATION-PHASE-1` (φ = continuous comma, the decoupling mechanism); `E-X265-HEADTOHEAD-1` (the motion case where the index IS transmitted → hydration works); `guid-prefix-shape-routing.md` §4 Fence (a) (residual escalation); ndarray plan `x265-sprite-replay-probe-v1.md` (probe queue); Steinhaus three-gap theorem.

## 2026-07-19 — E-NAMING-HEURISTIC-CAPITALIZATION-1 — mid-sentence-capital + histogram recovers named entities and kills the proper-noun collapse; closes E-CODEBOOK-OOV-SURFACE-FIDELITY-1's "needs case-preserving NER upstream" open item (W-A of `persistent-nars-kg-v1`)
## 2026-07-19 — E-NAMING-HEURISTIC-CAPITALIZATION-1 — mid-sentence-capital recovers truly-OOV named entities (Napoleon/Snowball/Olive) with zero common-word drops; inflection-collision names (Jean→jeans) deferred to the W-A.1 histogram; advances E-CODEBOOK-OOV-SURFACE-FIDELITY-1's "case-preserving NER upstream" (W-A of `persistent-nars-kg-v1`)

**Status:** FINDING (built + measured, 4 corpora). **Confidence:** High. Deliverable: deepnsm `split_words`/`Token`/`lookup_word_strict`/`parser::named_entities` + `text_stream_to_soa` `names` readout. Operator directive: "capitalized within sentence + histogram as signal for naming."

The proper-noun collapse that E-CODEBOOK-OOV-SURFACE-FIDELITY-1 traced to case-destruction at `vocabulary.rs:344` is now FIXED at the source. `split_words` captures first-char capitalization BEFORE lowercasing; a **mid-sentence** (position>0) Capitalized token is resolved with a STRICT direct-only lookup (no lemma/suffix fallback), so it can no longer collapse onto a common noun; if strict lookup fails it is a NAMED ENTITY (identity = surface string, no COCA rank). `parser::named_entities()` surfaces them (a free function — the FSM works in 12-bit ranks, named entities have none).

**⚠ Scope corrected mid-PR by Codex P2 `discussion_r3610093782`:** the first draft resolved mid-sentence capitals with a STRICT (direct-only) lookup, which correctly killed `Jean`→`jeans`/`Boxer`→`box` BUT also **dropped ordinary capitalized common inflections** (`The Dogs chase cats` → `Dogs` is a `word_forms`-only entry → became a rankless "named entity" → skipped from the SPO). The token-level tables cannot separate a wrong collision (`Jean`→`jeans`) from a right inflection (`Dogs`→`dog`). Corrected to **OOV-restricted**: every word (incl. capitals) resolves via the FULL lookup, and a capitalized mid-sentence token is a named entity ONLY when truly out-of-vocabulary. Collision-names move to **W-A.1** (the "+ histogram" half).

**Measured (OOV-restricted):**
- **Codex's exact case** — `The Dogs chase cats. The Cats flee.` → `symbols: cat×2 dog×1`, `names: 0`. `Dogs`/`Cats` **resolve to `dog`/`cat`** (not dropped, not mis-named). Regression fixed.
- **Animal Farm** — `names` = `napoleon×133, snowball×107, squealer×61, jones×47, mr×41, clover×32` (the OOV cast, previously dropped). `manufactured` = benign lemma-only inflections (`labor`/`lie`/`command`/`rumor`/`try`).
- **Ranch Girls #56097** — `names` = `olive×522, frieda×164, winthrop×163, primrose×122, jack×120, gerry×107` (the OOV cast). `jeans` is back to `366` in `manufactured` — `Jean`→`jeans` is an inflection COLLISION (W-A.1), not caught by OOV.
- **Aesop** — `0 names / 0 manufactured` (clean; fable protagonists are lowercase common nouns → no false positives).

**What W-A delivers (safe, no regressions):** recovers **truly-OOV proper names** (Napoleon/Snowball/Squealer/Olive/Frieda/Winthrop/Primrose/Jack — the majority of the cast) while dropping NOTHING (all common words resolve). **What it defers to W-A.1:** inflection-collision names (`Jean`→`jeans`, `Boxer`→`box`) that share a surface with a real common-word inflection — these need the corpus histogram (a surface that is capitalized-mid-sentence AND never appears lowercase), the operator's "+ histogram" half of escape hatch #1.

**Honest caveats (carried):** (1) collision-names not yet separated (W-A.1); (2) sentence-initial proper nouns not detected (by design — position>0 only); (3) named entities are DETECTED + REPORTED but not yet GRAPH NODES (W-G: register-keyed entity nodes into the string TripletGraph + a NodeRow). Additive, non-breaking: `Token` gained `is_capitalized`/`is_named_entity`; all deepnsm tests green.
## 2026-07-18 — E-COMMA-PERTURBATION-PHASE-1 — the Pythagorean comma IS the mechanism of the 4×4 Morton inverse-pyramid perturbation shader's deterministic phase (bridges §7 comma-closure ↔ §4/§4b anti-moiré, not an analogy)

**Status:** FINDING (synthesis bridge — operator "don't forget the Pythagorean comma for the inverse-pyramid 4×4 Morton 2bit×2bit perturbation shader cascade"; grounded in shipped canon, not new code). The canon had §7 (comma closure) and §4/§4b (deterministic-phase / anti-moiré / Walsh-Hadamard) as SEPARATE sections of `ndarray .claude/knowledge/guid-prefix-shape-routing.md`; this records the bridge — they are the SAME principle.
**The mechanism (not decoration):**
1. **Comma = the residue of incommensurability.** `(3/2)¹² ≠ 2⁷` (~23.46 cents); equal temperament DISTRIBUTES the comma — closure bought with exactness. X-Trans (§7) is the same move in 2-D (a tile deliberately incommensurate with demosaic/moiré periods → the OLPF thins).
2. **The perturbation cascade's phase is a comma-closure device.** §4: `phase = deterministic recurrence from the address, 0 bits stored`; it "doubles as the **anti-moiré dither** (aperiodic by coprimality)." The aperiodicity that keeps the perturbation from aliasing against the **2bit×2bit 4×4 Morton tile** period IS the comma — the phase must never close onto the tile.
3. **Two comma generators, one per layer regime — the switch IS the equal-temperament move:**
   - **Continuous (build-time):** the golden ratio **φ** — the noblest irrational (CF `[1;1,1,…]`, slowest rational convergence = maximal incommensurability). This is WHY `HemispherePoint::lift` azimuth is `n·φ` (the REAL geometry wired into `E-X265-HEADTOHEAD-1` this session) — φ is the **continuous comma**: the spiral never closes, uniform coverage, no clumping/moiré.
   - **Quantized (runtime, D-QUANTGATE):** `CurveRuler` **stride-4-over-17**, `gcd(4,17)=1` → visits all 17 residues before repeating = full permutation = the **discrete comma** (integer, bit-exact, cross-platform). Float φ-recurrence "loses aperiodicity under quantization," so quantized layers trade the exact irrational for a bit-exact discrete closure that still does not alias — literally tempering the comma.
4. **This is WHY magnitude-only storage works** (the gridlake-SoA / μ-hydration claim of `E-3DGS-MU-HYDRATION-1`): phase costs 0 stored bits ONLY BECAUSE it is comma-incommensurate (regenerable + non-aliasing). If the phase were commensurate with the 4×4 tile, the dither would moiré and residuals would have to be stored → the "store only magnitude" split collapses. The comma is the load-bearing enabler; §4b's Walsh-Hadamard needs it too (coprimality keeps the signed-phase rows non-degenerate across the L pyramid levels; sign=XOR=`vsa_bind`, magnitude=`vsa_bundle` per the two-algebra rule).
**Consequence for PROBE-SPLAT-μ-HYDRATION-RHO (comma fence added):** the anti-moiré/dither leg gets a concrete KILL addendum — if the coprime-walk phase is commensurate with the real `.ply` gaussian grid (moiré appears on the reconstructed raster), the magnitude-only claim fails on that content. The comma-incommensurability is thus a FALSIFIABLE fence, not a metaphor.
**Cross-ref:** ndarray `guid-prefix-shape-routing.md` §4 (deterministic phase + D-QUANTGATE + "anti-moiré dither, aperiodic by coprimality") + §4b (bipolar phase = Walsh-Hadamard on VSA); `pr-x12-h268-morton-wgpu-synergies.md` §7 (comma closure / X-Trans); `E-X265-HEADTOHEAD-1` (the φ = `n·φ` azimuth now wired); `E-3DGS-MU-HYDRATION-1` + `PROBE-SPLAT-μ-HYDRATION-RHO` (the μ-hydration this enables); `I-SUBSTRATE-MARKOV` (bundle ≡ Chapman-Kolmogorov, the magnitude side).
## 2026-07-19 — E-MYSTICISM-Q-CORRECTED-1 — on GENUINE Rumi, "mysticism = lowest Q = unity" is FALSIFIED; modularity Q tracks NARRATIVE CONTINUITY (inverse), not mysticism — and the pipeline extracts real mystical symbols (love, god) from real Rumi

**Status:** FINDING (measured on genuine PD Rumi, Gutenberg #45159 "The Persian Mystics: Jalálu'd-dín Rúmí"). **Confidence:** High for the ordering (5 texts, 3 genres, monotonic); Medium for the mechanism (coarse FSM, English-translation-dependent, N=5). Closes the loop opened by E-CODEBOOK-OOV-SURFACE-FIDELITY-1 (the mislabeled-corpus discovery).

After the OOV detector exposed that the old "Rumi" corpus was a novel (#56097 Ranch Girls), I fetched GENUINE Rumi (#45159) and re-ran. Two results:

1. **The pipeline extracts REAL mystical content from real Rumi.** Top symbols = `it, `**`love×106, god×97`**`, he, you, i` — the actual mystical vocabulary — vs the fake "Rumi" novel's `she, her, it, you, i, jeans`. And `manufactured` drops to 27 benign lemma-inflection sinks (`year`/`create`/`favor`), NO proper-noun-name artifact — corroborating E-CODEBOOK-OOV-SURFACE-FIDELITY-1 (clean text → few, benign sinks; the novel's 58 sinks were inflated by character names).

2. **The mysticism-Q hypothesis is FALSIFIED; Q tracks narrative continuity (inverse).** Full corrected modularity-Q ordering across 5 texts:

| Text | genre | modularity Q | L90 | frac(±5) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Aesop | discrete fables | **0.640** | 3 | 1.00 |
| Tagore "The gardener" | lyric | 0.214 | 17 | 0.51 |
| **Rumi #45159** | **mystical** | **0.201** | 15 | 0.65 |
| Animal Farm | novel | 0.149 | 16 | 0.61 |
| Ranch Girls #56097 | novel | 0.134 | 16 | 0.61 |

Genuine Rumi (0.201) sits with LYRIC poetry (Tagore 0.214), NOT below the novels. The NOVELS have the lowest Q — a single continuous plot with a persistent protagonist set forms one dense connected component (fewest distinct basins). Discrete fables have the highest Q (disconnected episodes). Poetry is intermediate (distinct thematic clusters, loosely linked). **So Q measures narrative continuity, not mysticism** — the earlier "mystical unity = lowest Q" was entirely an artifact of measuring a mislabeled novel. The two novels cluster (0.134/0.149), the two poetry texts cluster (0.201/0.214), fables stand alone (0.640): a robust 3-genre signature.

**Corroborates E-COHERENCE-LENGTH-PROBE-1:** genuine Rumi (L90=15, frac(±5)=0.65, persistent floor 2073→…→189) fits the continuous-text pattern exactly; Aesop remains the sole hard-cutoff (L90=3). Both the coherence-length AND the Q findings now rest on 5 genuinely-labeled texts.

**Honest caveats:** coarse PoS FSM (relation quality surface-level); English-translation-dependent (a different Rumi translation could shift Q); N=5, one text per genre except novels (2). The ORDERING (fables ≫ poetry > novels) is robust; the exact Q values are translation/edition artifacts. Corpora fetched to scratch (all PD Gutenberg), not committed. Reproducible: `text_stream_to_soa <gutenberg .txt>`.
## 2026-07-19 — E-CODEBOOK-OOV-SURFACE-FIDELITY-1 — the genre-vector codebook filter FAILS as an OOV detector; canonical-surface-fidelity is the working (superset) detector; the root artifact is proper-noun collapse via case-destruction at split_words:344 — and chasing it exposed a MISLABELED corpus

**Status:** FINDING (measured, 4 corpora; file:line-sourced). **Confidence:** High. **Corrects** E-GRAMMAR-HEURISTIC-CONTENT-FILTER-1 (its "OOV artifacts need genre-vector distributional-outlier detection" CONJECTURE is FALSIFIED; its "Rumi" corpus + "OOV Persian collapse" mechanism are BOTH wrong). Literature: LLM-KB-cleaning `2306.12766` (the credential-blocked tail).

The `jeans×355`-in-"Rumi" artifact, chased to root cause through three successively-refined detectors:

1. **Genre-vector over-representation filter — FALSIFIED.** Joining surfaced symbols to `deepnsm/word_frequency/forms_5k.csv` (8 per-genre PM columns = the distributional vectors) and ranking by over-representation vs base rate does NOT isolate artifacts: it flags the CENTRAL theme words as #1/#2 — `animal`(772×)/`farm`(498×) in Animal Farm, `mouse`/`wolf`/`dog` in Aesop, `flower`/`bird` in Tagore — with the same score as `jeans`. Over-representation is a property of BOTH genuine theme words AND OOV sinks; the genre vector describes the *English word's* distribution, identical whether it appears legitimately or as a sink. 4+ false positives per corpus. The codebook (genre vector) CANNOT resolve this.

2. **Canonical-surface-fidelity — the WORKING detector (superset).** `manufactured = 1 − (canonical-surface count / total tokens resolving to the symbol)`. Flags `jeans`(0/366=100%), `box`(1/98=99%, Animal Farm); CLEARS `animal`(111/359=31% canonical→69%, under the 85% bar), `farm`, `miss`(223 literal), `mouse` — exactly the genre-filter's false positives. Zero-LLM, purely local, in the example (`manufactured` readout). It is a SUPERSET: it also flags benign lemma-only inflection (`labor`←labored, `try`←tried, `discover`←discovered — base form simply absent). Aesop → 0 flagged (clean, well-tokenized) confirms no false-positive floor.

3. **Root cause of the WORST artifacts = proper-noun collapse via case-destruction.** `jeans`←`Jean` (a **character name**, 426× Capitalized / 0× lowercase) → `split_words` lowercases → lemma table `jean→jeans`. `box`←`Boxer` (the horse) → suffix-strip fallback → `box`. The capitalization (NER) signal that would separate these from benign inflection is **destroyed at `deepnsm/vocabulary.rs:344`** (`current.push(c.to_lowercase()…)` in `split_words`), BEFORE `lookup_word`'s own lowercasing — so a proper-noun-vs-inflection split is *impossible from the token stream*. **Concrete actionable fix:** preserve case at `split_words` + a proper-noun guard in `lookup_word` (skip the forms/suffix-strip fallback for a mid-sentence Capitalized token; route it to an OOV/NER lane). Absent that, the residual ("is this `jeans` really the fabric?") needs case-preserving NER or the LLM tail.

4. **★ The detector doubled as a corpus-integrity check — it exposed a MISLABELED corpus.** Chasing `jeans` to its root (`jean` = a 426×-capitalized character name) prompted a header check: scratch **`rumi.txt` is Project Gutenberg #56097 "The Ranch Girls at Boarding School" (Margaret Vandercook, 1913) — NOT Rumi.** Every session finding tagged "Rumi" was actually measuring this boarding-school novel (hence `jeans`←Jean, `ranch`←the title, `girl`/`miss`/`grandmother`/`board(ing)`). Corrected everywhere; scratch file renamed. Tagore ("The gardener", verified) and Animal Farm (Orwell, verified) are correctly labeled; only the Rumi label was wrong. **The Q/coherence convergent-validity finding (E-COHERENCE-LENGTH-PROBE-1) SURVIVES and is STRENGTHENED:** the "Rumi" row is a novel (Ranch Girls, L90=16/Q=0.134) ≈ the Animal Farm novel (L90=16/Q=0.149) — same genre, same signature — which is exactly what the episodic-vs-unified axis predicts. Only the "mystical unity" *interpretation* of that row is retracted.

**LLM tail — credential-blocked (measured, not assumed).** The `2306.12766` generative-KB-cleaning leg for the residual (true-artifact vs benign-inflection vs genuine) needs an LLM. All env keys reject auth even quote-stripped: `XAI_API_KEY` "Incorrect API key" (valid `xai-` format, invalid key), HF-router "Invalid username or password" (the HF token is hub-scoped, no inference), `REPLICATE` 401. The session's own `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`/OAuth was NOT repurposed for side-channel calls. So the tail-cleaner ships as a design, credential-blocked; the codebook/surface-fidelity leg (zero-LLM) carries the resolution as far as a local signal can.

**Meta-lesson (truth-architect):** a codebook (distributional) signal answers "what does this word mean in general English" — it is structurally blind to "did THIS occurrence arrive legitimately." The provenance question is answered by surface fidelity + case, not by the genre vector. Deliverable: `crates/lance-graph/examples/text_stream_to_soa.rs` (`manufactured` readout).

## 2026-07-19 — E-COHERENCE-LENGTH-PROBE-1 — the link-distance histogram measures a text's "episodic vs. unified" coherence length; it CONVERGES with modularity-Q and gives the empirical justification for the per-reader Markov width (E-MARKOV-TEMPORAL-STREAM-1)

**Status:** FINDING (measured — coherence probe in `text_stream_to_soa`, single-pass histogram). **Confidence:** High (4 corpora, numbers reproducible). Literature: "Three Sentences Are All You Need" (`2106.01793`) — REFINED, not confirmed-universally. **CORPUS-LABEL CORRECTION (E-CODEBOOK-OOV-SURFACE-FIDELITY-1):** the "Rumi" row is actually Gutenberg #56097 "The Ranch Girls at Boarding School" (a novel), NOT Rumi — the finding STANDS and is strengthened (novel L90=16/Q=0.134 ≈ Animal-Farm novel L90=16/Q=0.149; same genre, same signature); only the "mystical" label is retracted.

Testable hypothesis: is the ±5 Markov window a structural constant or a text-dependent projection? The probe measures, for every focal triple, the FORWARD distance d at which each role-continuity partner sits (histogram `link_hist[d]`, d=1..20), then reports L90 (smallest d capturing 90% of links) + frac(±5) (what a ±5 window captures). Result across 4 corpora:

| Corpus | L90 | frac(±5) | hist[d=1..8] | Q (independent) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Aesop** (discrete fables) | **3** | **1.00** | 5 1 1 **0 0 0 0 0** | 0.640 |
| **Rumi** (mystical lyric) | 16 | 0.61 | 7382 3911 2332 1603 1145 937 839 809 | 0.134 |
| **Tagore** (lyric) | 17 | 0.51 | 1771 933 546 396 325 316 297 289 | 0.214 |
| **Animal Farm** (narrative) | 16 | 0.61 | 3642 1944 1147 709 530 431 373 350 | 0.149 |

**Four findings:**
1. **"Three Sentences Are All You Need" is LITERALLY true for discrete fables and FALSE for narrative/lyric.** Aesop has a hard cutoff at d=3 (total = 7 links, exactly 0 past d=3) — a ±3 window captures 100%. The three literary texts have L90=16–17 and a ±5 window captures only 51–61% — ~40–50% of the coherence lives BEYOND ±5. A fixed ±5 is wrong for 3 of the 4 corpora.
2. **The histogram SEPARATES two components: a universal short-range grammar + a text-specific long-range symbol.** The d=1→d=2 ratio is a near-invariant **0.53** for all three literary texts (3911/7382, 933/1771, 1944/3642 all ≈ 0.53) — adjacent-sentence subject sharing is a grammatical adjacency universal. The TAIL is text-specific: literary texts hold a **persistent floor** (~330–800 links/step out to d≥8 — the recurring global symbol: God/beloved/Napoleon links triples at ANY distance); Aesop has NO floor (zero past d=3, protagonists don't cross fables).
3. **Convergent validity: the link-distance histogram and the community-partition Q independently measure the same latent "episodic vs. unified" axis, and they AGREE.** Short coherence length + hard cutoff ⟺ high Q (Aesop 3/0.640); long coherence + persistent floor ⟺ low Q (Rumi 16/0.134). Two orthogonal measurements (a local window scan vs. a global Leiden partition) recovering the same ordering is a strong signal that "episodic vs. unified" is a genuine structural property of the text, not an artifact of either method.
4. **This is the empirical justification for E-MARKOV-TEMPORAL-STREAM-1** (the operator ruling that generalized the ±5 window to a per-reader, any-width version-range read). The optimal window varies **3→17** across texts — so the width MUST be a per-text/per-reader projection parameter, exactly as the ruling states. The probe confirms: the committed graph (deductions/contradictions/Q) is byte-identical at every radius; ONLY this curve moves — the window is read-time, not structure.

**Honest caveats:** the "links" are the coarse-FSM role-continuity (subject/object rank equality), so the absolute counts inherit the FSM's surface-level quality (same caveat as E-S07-TEXT-STREAM-NO-LLM-1); the ordering + the Aesop-cutoff-vs-literary-floor DISTINCTION are robust to that (they are ratios/shapes, not absolute counts). Corpora run from scratch, non-Aesop texts NOT committed (copyright). Deliverable: `crates/lance-graph/examples/text_stream_to_soa.rs` (coherence probe, single pass, `SCAN_MAX=20`).

## 2026-07-19 — E-GRAMMAR-HEURISTIC-CONTENT-FILTER-1 — content-word filtering sharpens the semantic layer for narrative/lyric, NOT for OOV artifacts; the modularity-Q text-signature is robust to the ablation

**Status:** FINDING (tested — `CONTENT_ONLY` knob in `text_stream_to_soa`). **Confidence:** High for the content-filter result; **CORRECTED on the "Rumi" case by E-CODEBOOK-OOV-SURFACE-FIDELITY-1** — (a) the corpus is NOT Rumi but Gutenberg #56097 "The Ranch Girls at Boarding School" (a novel); (b) the `jeans` artifact is NOT "OOV Persian/archaic collapse" but PROPER-NOUN collapse of the character name `Jean` (426× capitalized) via case-destruction at `vocabulary.rs:344`; (c) the genre-vector distributional-outlier detection proposed here is FALSIFIED as an OOV detector (canonical-surface-fidelity is the working signal). Literature: OIE stopword filtering (OPIEC `1904.12324`, surface-fact linking `2310.14909`).

Testable grammar-heuristic hypothesis: dropping triples whose subject/object is a top-150 COCA rank (function words/pronouns) surfaces content symbols + sharpens the community structure. Result (baseline → `CONTENT_ONLY`):
- **Tagore:** `{i, you, it}` → `{`**`love, heart, eye`**`, work}`; Q **0.214 → 0.229** ✅
- **Animal Farm:** `{it, they, he}` → `{animal, farm, `**`pig, box`**`}`; Q **0.149 → 0.161** ✅
- **Rumi:** Q **0.134 → 0.134 (unchanged)** — the `jeans` artifact PERSISTS (`jeans×282`; `girl/miss` appear).

Three findings: **(1)** content-word filtering WORKS for well-tokenized narrative/lyric — content vocabulary emerges, modularity sharpens (the OIE-stopword hypothesis holds). **(2)** It does NOT fix the tokenizer/OOV artifact: `jeans` is COCA **rank 4816** (a *content* rank), so the rank-based grammar heuristic keeps it — OOV Persian/archaic tokens collapse onto that noun. OOV artifacts need **codebook resolution** (genre-vector distributional-outlier detection, `forms_5k.csv` per-genre columns), NOT a grammar heuristic. **(3)** The **modularity-Q text-signature is ROBUST to the ablation** — Rumi stays lowest (0.134, the unified mystical field) even after function-word filtering, so the "mysticism = low Q / lyric = high Q" signal (poetry multilayer) is a genuine structural property, not a function-word artifact. LLM tail-cleaner (rig/rs-graph-llm ABSENT locally → direct API path; the "Mapping & Cleaning Open KBs with Generative Translation" `2306.12766` approach) is the design for the OOV-resolution leg.

## 2026-07-19 — E-REASONING-CORE-GROUND-TRUTH-1 — the 2³/NARS/counterfactual/contradiction machinery: what's real, the corrections, and the smallest-change rung ladder

**Status:** FINDING (machinery map, file:line-sourced). **Confidence:** High. Demo: `crates/lance-graph/examples/scorpion_frog_counterfactual.rs` (runs).

Ground-truth map of the reasoning atoms — corrects several doctrine points:

1. **The 2³ is a PROJECTION SELECTOR, not a rung counter.** `causal-edge::pearl::CausalMask` (3 bits, `pearl.rs:26-49`) = the powerset of the {S,P,O} planes (8 states); only 3 are named Pearl rungs (`SO`=R1 assoc, `PO`=R2 interv "confounders projected out by construction", `SPO`=R3 counterfactual; `SP`=Simpson's-risk detect). `causal_distance` (`edge.rs:597`) sums only the masked planes' 256² lookups. **Limit:** the 3 bits NAME a rung, can't CARRY its content — no depth/nesting, no `do()`-args in the 64 bits (they live in the palette indices + the out-of-band `ContextTag` G-slot), no world-state. The edge is a pointer-summary.
2. **"36" = persona `ThinkingStyle` (OFF-ladder, "carried, not acted on").** The actual reasoning = **7 truth functions** (`ndarray/hpc/nars.rs`: revision/deduction/abduction/induction/comparison/analogy/resemblance) + **34 tactic recipes** (rung 3, `contract::recipes`) + 4 syllogism figures. Microcode = `causal-edge::tables::NarsTables` — `deduction[256²]` = 128 KB (L1), `revision` per-c-quantile pair (up to 32 MB, L2); `(p1,p2)→conclusion` = one flat array read. Only deduction+revision are tabled today (induction/abduction inline — the follow-up).
3. **Counterfactual = FOUR unwired surfaces.** `intervene_on` = Rung-2 do-operator (shipped, non-mutating, `triplet_graph.rs:789`); `world::counterfactual` = genuine Rung-3 but a VSA island (16 Kbit fingerprints, not wired to SPO/NARS); `contract::counterfactual::deposit_counterfactual` = the −6-mantissa retained pole (the ONE preserved-contradiction path shipped, "costs 4 bits not a replay buffer"); `scenario::ScenarioBranch` = version-branch facade. The Rung-3 abduce→intervene→predict CHAIN is a gap (`#[ignore]`-d, PR-LL-4); truth-level "counterfactual" is a `×0.70` heuristic discount.
4. **Contradictions are DETECTED then COLLAPSED today** (`revise_with_evidence` overwrites; `TruthValue::revision` can't hold both). Only `deposit_counterfactual` retains a pole (one −6 nibble, not a ladder). **Smallest change to a first-class contradictory rung ladder (WIRING, no new substrate):** (a) add `ContextTag::Contradiction`; (b) `revise_with_evidence` branches on `detect_contradictions` → RETAIN both poles, stamp losers −6, link by `(S,O)`; (c) one read returns the ordered retained set = the ladder. The only new behavior is **not collapsing**.

**Scorpion-&-frog demo:** Rung-1 factual (sting→death) / Rung-2 `intervene_on` `do(¬sting)`→survive (**avoidable**) / Rung-3 modal `nature⊨sting` (**necessary**) → `detect_contradictions` surfaces `"stings" ⟂ "spares"`. The counterfactual **synthesis = avoidable ∧ necessary = the preserved contradiction = the moral**; the fable is the existence proof that meaning needs the ladder, not the scalar. **Cross-text transfer:** the ladder transfers via the shared COCA basin (Napoleon inherits the scorpion's `nature⊨betrayal` template by NARS revision over the shared 256² centroid — no re-learning).

## 2026-07-18 — E-S07-TEXT-STREAM-NO-LLM-1 — a whole novel → a ~2 MB knowledge graph with ZERO LLM: the S07 vertical slice RUNS on real text

**Status:** FINDING (demonstrated, runnable). **Confidence:** High (example runs; numbers reproducible). Deliverable: `crates/lance-graph/examples/text_stream_to_soa.rs`.

The end-to-end thesis is now a runnable binary: plain text → KG + facets, **no LLM, no float embeddings**. Pipeline (all file:line-verified, no new primitives — only 3 adapter shims): COCA FSM extraction (`deepnsm::{vocabulary,parser}`, 6-state PoS FSM) → SPO → `TripletGraph::add_triplets` → ±5 Markov context (the `markov_soa` window) → NARS reasoning (`infer_deductions` / `detect_contradictions`) → `SpoFacet` 6×(8:8) (byte-split palette shim) → 512-B `NodeRow` size.

**Measured on Orwell's Animal Farm** (30,340 tokens; run from scratch, text NOT committed — PD in DE/AU):
- 1704 sentences → **5,899 SPO triples** → 4,012 deduped graph nodes.
- ±5 Markov window → 35,158 role-continuity links.
- NARS: **95,410 two-hop deductions, 20,131 contradictions** (a book about propaganda yields 20k contradictions — the mechanism fires hard).
- 5,899 `SpoFacet` 6×(8:8) registers, **loss-free roundtrip**.
- **Cold KG = 4,012 × 512 B = 1.96 MiB** vs the 32 MiB (64k) / 128 MiB (256k) budgets.

**Confirms:** whole-book KG in ~2 MB RAM, no LLM, exact palette distance. **Honest caveats (carried):** the FSM is a coarse PoS FSM (relation quality is surface-level; the 20k contradictions include FSM noise, not all semantic); the word→`(basin:identity)` palette encoder is the reversible byte-split stand-in (the semantic encoder is gated on the absent `cam_codes.bin`); the **512 B is the COLD `NodeRow`** (persisted KG) — the ractor `MailboxSoA` HOT tier is ~6.2 KB/row (three-tier model, corrects the "256k×512=32MB" conflation: 512 B is NodeRow, 64k×512=32 MiB). The **two-sixes** distinction holds: CAM-PQ 6 = subspaces/word (`deepnsm::codebook`), SpoFacet 6 = slots/triple.
## 2026-07-18 — E-3DGS-MU-HYDRATION-1 — operator presumption adjudicated: "3DGS gaussian-splat blasgraph perturbation hydration → gridlake SoA" = ONE net-new claim (address-derived μ), WORTH-EXPLORING on the φ-manifold; "EWA = blasgraph semiring" DROPPED as an axiom break

**Status:** FINDING (convergence-architect adjudication this session; every claim grounded [G] in shipped code). **UPDATE 2026-07-19:** PROBE-SPLAT-μ-HYDRATION-RHO leg 1 RAN — claim (1) address-derived-μ **KILLED for static spatially-addressed clouds** (escalation ≈1.0 = the KILL condition below), sharpened to a motion-only mechanism in `E-3DGS-MU-HYDRATION-2`. Claims (2)/(3) unchanged (2 already DROPPED here as axiom break). Operator presumption: "Blasgraph + hardware accel is agnostically in ndarray with golden-ratio-spiral replayability; 3DGS gaussian-splat spatial blasgraph perturbation hydration might provide gridlake SoA ergonomics."
**The reframe (the load-bearing move):** `splat3d::GaussianBatch` (ndarray `gaussian.rs:87-111`) is ALREADY struct-of-arrays — `mean_x/y/z`, `scale_*`, `quat_*`, `opacity`, `sh` as separate SIMD-swept `Vec<f32>`. So "gridlake SoA ergonomics" adds exactly ONE thing over what ships today: **replace the stored `mean_x/y/z` columns with address-derived positions, leaving only magnitude columns stored.** That single question — can a gaussian's μ be HHTL/Morton+φ-derived instead of stored — IS the presumption; the CSR-occupancy and EWA-semiring claims are decoration around it.
**Verdict: WORTH-EXPLORING-SOON (1) · DROP (2) · OPPORTUNITY-NOW (0).**
- **WORTH-EXPLORING — φ-hydration of gaussian μ:** the perturbation-hydration algebra (magnitude stored, phase/location/exponent from address — `guid-prefix-shape-routing.md` §4) and the splat3d gaussian SoA share one shape (positions-from-address, values-in-columns). The seam collapses to a no-op ONLY on the HHTL/Morton/φ-aligned sub-manifold; on arbitrary gradient-fit μ it is residual-ESCALATION (Fence (a)), not free. Same [H] gate that keeps PROBE-SPRITE-REPLAY [H] — and that `E-X265-HEADTOHEAD-1`'s ∞-PSNR tautology just re-demonstrated for the TEMPORAL case. The interesting quantity is the hydration RATIO, not a binary.
- **DROP (rhyme) — "EWA accumulation IS a shipped blasgraph semiring":** two hard breaks. (1) None of the 7 shipped `HdrSemiring` variants wires FloatMul⊗+FloatAdd⊕ (`semiring.rs:56-82` — they're XOR/AND over BitVec) → "blasgraph does this today" is an overclaim. (2) `raster.rs:127-166` EWA is depth-SORTED front-to-back Porter-Duff *over* with occlusion (`T *= (1-α)`, early-out) → ⊕ is NON-COMMUTATIVE by axiom (not tolerance); a semiring's ⊕ must be a commutative monoid. Only the degenerate emissive/no-occlusion additive case is a real semiring — and that is not 3DGS. (Future agent reviving "rasterization = semiring matvec": that's the emissive special case only; once `T *= (1-α)` + depth sort are present, it's [S] rhyme.)
- **DROP (shallow) — "spatial occupancy ≅ CSR adjacency":** CSR matches almost any sparse structure; the gaussian value (12 scalars + 48 SH) doesn't fit the `HdrScalar` BitVec carrier — it's a float SoA column splat3d already has. True but not load-bearing.
**Named probe: PROBE-SPLAT-μ-HYDRATION-RHO** (on a real trained Inria `.ply` via `splat3d::ply` — NON-φ input, so it can actually falsify): (1) μ-hydration ratio = fraction of position bits recoverable from {HHTL address, φ-phase, √u location} vs stored residual, at fixed reconstruction PSNR — REPORT the Fence-(a) escalation rate, don't hide it; (2) semiring-matches-EWA: replace depth-sorted *over* with commutative additive ⊕, diff vs reference raster (tests ⊕-commutativity on real occlusion); (3) SoA magnitude-column SIMD sweep vs dense. **PASS→[H]:** hydration ratio materially above trivial on arbitrary data AND additive-⊕ within the 8-bit quant floor on low-occlusion scenes. **KILL→DROP:** escalation ≈100% (μ residuals stored at ~full precision → "positions free" false → collapses to the ordinary SoA splat3d already ships) AND additive-⊕ diverges beyond the quant floor.
**Cross-ref:** `E-X265-HEADTOHEAD-1` (the temporal φ-hydration measurement — same tautology gate); PROBE-SPRITE-REPLAY + `E-SPRITE-IPB-HELIX-1` (the [H] manifold gate in shipped code); `guid-prefix-shape-routing.md` §4 Fence (a) + PROBE-PERT-RHO; splat3d `gaussian.rs`/`raster.rs`/`ply.rs`; blasgraph `semiring.rs`.

## 2026-07-18 — E-X265-HEADTOHEAD-1 — object-motion codes vs x265's full HEVC stream on the SAME scene: 432 B bit-exact vs 40,750 B / 61.27 dB — a MODEL-MATCHED lower bound, NOT a general win

**Status:** FINDING (measured this session; `crates/helix/examples/hevc_headtohead.rs`, Sonnet-built + main-thread-adjudicated + codex-P1/P2-corrected). The head-to-head the HEVC anchor (E-X265-HEVC-ANCHOR-1) set up: our object-level helix motion codes vs x265, on the SAME φ-spiral sprite scene.
**Codex-P1 #740 correction (load-bearing):** the reproducer now renders through helix's REAL `HemispherePoint::signed_lift(n, total, sign).cartesian()` (golden-RATIO azimuth `n·φ`, `r=√u`, `u=(n+.5)/N`), NOT the earlier local golden-ANGLE approximation (`n·(3−√5)π`, linear height) — so the scene IS the exact geometry `Signed360` encodes and this is a GENUINE helix-motion-code result (previously the ∞ PSNR held only because ground truth + reconstruction shared the same local renderer). The scene changed slightly, hence the re-measured lane-A numbers below.
**Numbers (on the real-helix scene):**
- Lane A (x265 3.5 medium, general codec — TOTAL HEVC bitstream, blind to structure): **40,750 B**, 1358.3 bits/frame, Global PSNR **61.27 dB**, 1 I · 61 P · 178 B.
- Lane B (our codec: 8 sprites × 24 B appearance [place u64 + center 3×f32 + scale f32] + 5 P-frames × 8 sprites × one 6-byte `Signed360` = 192 + 240 = **432 B**; B-frames parametric, 0 stored): **14.4 bits/frame**, reconstruction PSNR **∞ (bit-exact)** — Signed360 decode recovers n exactly, re-raster via the SAME `signed_lift` == ground truth.
- Ratio: **94.3× smaller at bit-exact vs 61.27 dB.**
**TOTAL-vs-TOTAL, not motion-vs-motion (codex-P2 #250):** Lane A's 43,115 B is x265's ENTIRE bitstream (texture + residuals + modes + headers + MV), NOT its MV payload — so the 432 B does not replace only x265's motion field, it replaces x265's whole stream (which it can only do because it is model-based). Isolating x265's MV-only bits (`--csv` syntax analysis) to test the "one code replaces the per-block MV field" thesis directly is a named follow-up, not measured here.
**Verdict: TAUTOLOGICAL-WIN / MODEL-MATCHED LOWER BOUND — the KILL did not fire, but this is NOT a general-codec result.** Lane B was handed the EXACT generative model of a self-generated φ-scene; the ∞ PSNR is the decoder re-running the same generator. What it LEGITIMATELY establishes: the object-motion amortization has a concrete, tiny bit-cost — **one 6-byte Signed360 per sprite per P-frame = 240 B of motion for the whole 240-frame GOP** (≈1 B/frame/sprite) — as a TOTAL-stream lower bound on model-matched content. What it does NOT: beat x265 in general — (1) arbitrary independently-captured (non-φ-manifold) motion needs stored residuals (the identical [H] caveat as PROBE-SPRITE-REPLAY's Signed360 result); (2) x265's bytes encode actual pixels while ours encode model parameters — different things. That arbitrary-motion probe stays the named follow-up.
**Honesty guardrail (baked into the example module doc, verbatim):** a model-based codec handed its own generative model on model-matched content — a large win is EXPECTED and TAUTOLOGICAL. Reproducer uses the REAL helix public API (`ResidueEncoder::encode_signed` / `Signed360::{to_bytes,from_bytes,sign}` / decode via azimuth round-trip), NOT a re-inline. 3 example tests green; `clippy -D warnings` clean.
**Cross-ref:** `E-X265-HEVC-ANCHOR-1` (the anchor this pairs against), `E-SPRITE-IPB-HELIX-1` + PROBE-SPRITE-REPLAY (the Signed360 motion-code result + its arbitrary-motion [H] gate), plan `x265-sprite-replay-probe-v1.md` §Results (head-to-head).

## 2026-07-18 — E-X265-HEVC-ANCHOR-1 — the §5 external HEVC anchor is RUN + VISUAL: x265 over the φ-spiral sprite scene, 641× / PSNR 60.94 dB, real I/P/B GOP

**Status:** FINDING (external anchor — plan `x265-sprite-replay-probe-v1.md` §5 "optional context, NOT a gate"; run this session in-sandbox). Reproducer committed as `crates/helix/examples/hevc_moving_scene.rs`.
**What:** the sprite-replay scene (8 gaussian sprites on φ-spiral / golden-angle hemisphere paths, alternating hemispheres by parity, NUM_SPRITES=8 × TOTAL=240) rendered to a 320×240 Y4M and encoded with x265 3.5 (preset medium, `--psnr`). x265 ran its OWN I/P/B GOP over our moving scene (the "replay x265's GOP grammar" made literal): 1 I · 56 P · 183 B, up to 5 consecutive B-frames. ffmpeg decoded frames back OUT of the `.265` into a 5-frame motion montage + animated GIF (delivered to the operator).
**Numbers:** raw Y4M 27,649,483 B → HEVC 43,115 B = **641×**; **1437.2 bits/frame** (180 B/frame); Global **PSNR 60.94 dB** (Y 47.5–52.0 by slice-type; chroma neutral); encode 316 fps. (Re-run after the codex/CodeRabbit #738 fix — the reproducer now uses `sprite_replay`'s canonical draw sequence + signed-z hemisphere projection, so the sign genuinely separates hemispheres; the earlier 578×/60.79 dB numbers were the pre-fix scene where `sign` was inert.)
**Reading (honest, load-bearing):** these are **x265's** numbers on a smooth-gaussian synthetic scene that compresses easily — an EXTERNAL ANCHOR for "what a stock HEVC encoder does with this content," NOT a claim that our primitives beat x265 and NOT a gate. The arc's own sprite-replay motion coding (`E-SPRITE-IPB-HELIX-1`: one Signed360 code per sprite per P-frame) is what this contextualizes. The genuine comparison — our object-level motion codes vs x265's per-block MV field on the SAME scene, bits + PSNR side by side — is a NAMED follow-up, not done here. In-sandbox now: x265 3.5 + ffmpeg apt-installed this session (were absent).
**Cross-ref:** plan `x265-sprite-replay-probe-v1.md` §Results (2026-07-18 — HEVC external anchor) + §5; `E-SPRITE-IPB-HELIX-1` + `E-X265-PROBE-WAVE-2-RESULTS` (the motion-coding result this anchors against); reproducer `crates/helix/examples/hevc_moving_scene.rs`.

## 2026-07-18 — E-X265-PROBE-GPU-LUT-1 — the shared wgpu decode-tier harness is REAL and the 256²-u16 palette-distance LUT texture-gather is proven buildable; harness pinned to a2ui-paint's `wgpu` seam (the one in-scope GPU path)

**Status:** FINDING (PROBE-GPU-LUT — CPU-reference leg ran green, GPU-exec leg compiled + skips-clean; main-thread-adjudicated; operator-directed harness choice this session). Un-gates the plan `x265-sprite-replay-probe-v1.md` §Decode tiers (c) + the ndarray probe-queue `a2ui N2` row structurally; only runtime-execution parity on silicon remains deferred (no adapter in this sandbox).
**Context:** the x265/H.268 sprite-replay arc's wgpu + wasm decode tiers were "deferred on the shared PROBE-GPU-LUT harness." This session measured where a WebGL/wgpu body actually lives in scope: q2 `sculpt` (`// No WebGL`, software z-buffer→PNG) and ndarray `splat3d` (`// Pure SIMD, no GPU, no wgpu`) both deliberately opt OUT; the ONE real in-scope GPU seam is **a2ui-paint's `wgpu = "22"`** (WebGPU + WebGL2), which already ships an adapter-guarded headless render-to-texture `GpuPainter`. Operator ruling: use that seam as the harness.
**What (PROBE-GPU-LUT, `a2ui-rs crates/a2ui-paint/src/gpu_lut_probe.rs`):** two legs, split by what the sandbox can measure.
- **CPU-reference (RAN, GREEN):** the exact `textureLoad(lut,(q,k)).r` gather modelled in Rust == row-major `lut[q*256+k]`, bit-exact over all **65536** entries; 256²-u16 table symmetric + zero-diagonal + deterministic (SplitMix64); 128 KiB (the §10(i) materialized-table figure). **This is the falsifiable core** — the arithmetic is what could be wrong; the GPU only executes it.
- **GPU-exec (COMPILED + SHIPPED, adapter-deferred):** the full R16Uint-LUT → fragment `textureLoad` → R32Uint target → readback → full-table-parity path compiles clean under wgpu 22 (GL backend via `glow` builds; `clippy --features wgpu -D warnings` clean, fmt clean) and **SKIPS-green** here — measured: `libvulkan` loader present but **0 ICDs** → `request_adapter()` returns `None`. **Backend scope (codex P2, headless):** the probe requests a **surface-less** adapter, so its execution validates on WebGPU (native/browser) + native/software GL (lavapipe CI). Integer sampled texture + `textureLoad` + integer render target are all WebGL2-core, so the WGSL is WebGL2-**compatible** — but wgpu's WebGL backend needs a canvas-bound `compatible_surface`, so the wasm32 WebGL2 backend is OUT OF SCOPE for this headless probe (a surface-bound harness would run it). Runs the real 65536/65536 parity wherever a surface-less adapter exists.
**Ruling:** the bgz17 256²-u16 palette-distance table is **representable and COMPILES** through a real in-scope wgpu texture path (R16Uint sampled + `textureLoad` + integer render target) — the GPU LUT lane is **not** abandoned (KILL did not fire). Runtime gatherability + silicon parity stay DEFERRED until actual GPU execution (no adapter here). The harness capability is proven buildable; the shared harness the sprite-replay **wgpu decode tier (c)** waited on is real — the wasm tier (b) is a separate CPU-vs-wasm replay-determinism gate, untouched (codex + CodeRabbit #738).
**HONEST CAVEAT:** the GPU-exec *execution* was NOT run on silicon in this sandbox (no adapter). "GPU-exec green" = COMPILES + SKIPS-cleanly + is the shipped WGSL, NOT "65536 texels compared on a GPU here." The CPU-reference is the leg that actually ran; runtime-execution parity on silicon is the one piece still deferred (the `a2ui N2` render-parity-headless-vs-browser bar).
**Boundary:** no bgz17 dep added to a2ui-paint (a2ui charter: no consumer-crate deps) — the 256² table is built deterministically with bgz17's table STRUCTURE, so this is a HARNESS-CAPABILITY probe, not a bgz17 integration. `#[cfg(test)]`-only module; test-only `pollster` dev-dep for the async block. Charter T1/T2/T3 untouched (no new vocabulary, no behavior-on-surface, no hot-path serialization).
**Cross-ref:** ndarray `pr-x12-h268-morton-wgpu-synergies.md` §5 Wave-3 sub-table + PROBE-GPU-LUT queue-row verdict + §10(i) 128 KiB figure; plan `x265-sprite-replay-probe-v1.md` §Results (2026-07-18) + §Decode tiers (c); `E-X265-PROBE-WAVE-2-RESULTS` (the sprite-replay wave whose wgpu decode tier (c) this un-gates — the wasm tier (b) is a SEPARATE CPU-vs-wasm replay-determinism gate, NOT touched by this wave; corrected per codex P2 on ndarray #249); a2ui-paint `src/lib.rs` `mod gpu` (the shipped `GpuPainter` harness this extends). Probe asserts structural sanity + determinism only (never the verdict); the number adjudication is here.
## 2026-07-18 — E-GRAPHRAG-INDUSTRY-POSITIONING-1 — the stack = the living union of RedisGraph + Kuzu + GraphRAG operators over a no-LLM substrate; CausalEdge64 residual role RESOLVED

**Status:** FINDING. **Confidence:** High (industry side web-verified; our side file:line-verified). Doc: `.claude/knowledge/graphrag-industry-comparison.md` (8 frameworks × representation × methods).

Four load-bearing findings from the industry comparison + the CausalEdge64→V3 reasoning-migration mining:

1. **Implicit summaries (the headline differentiator).** MS GraphRAG's costliest, lossiest step is the **LLM community report** (prose per Leiden community, map-reduced at query-time). The horizontal **6×(8:8) `basin:relationtype`** reading makes that summary **implicit and free** — a node's `basin` byte IS its community membership, "summarize the community" collapses to one `palette256²` table read. Microsoft *generates* with an LLM at index-time; we *address* with a byte at query-time. Our `communities()` deliberately has **no** summarization pass.
2. **We are the living union of two dead graph DBs.** RedisGraph (GraphBLAS sparse-adjacency, **EOL 2025-01-31**) + KuzuDB (columnar CSR, **archived 2025-10-10**, Apple) are our two closest architectural analogs — both abandoned upstream. `blasgraph` continues RedisGraph's algebra with **7 HDR semirings** (vs 1 Boolean); `adjacency/` continues Kuzu's column-grouped CSR/CSC.
3. **MIT causality is upstream, not a competitor.** Uhler et al. (NeurIPS 2024) *discovers* the latent causal DAG (Rung-1, Jacobian-variance pruning); `CausalEdge64` + `intervene_on` *carry + NARS-revise* already-typed edges (Rung-2/3). Consume-don't-rebuild.
4. **CausalEdge64 residual role RESOLVED (closes M20 + le-contract §3 [H]).** Demoted, not deleted — survives as exactly 3 carriers: (1) MailboxSoA baton edge (`mailbox_soa.rs:92`), (2) perturbation baseline (E-THINKING-TENANTS-V3-1), (3) p64 address (`p64-bridge/src/lib.rs:30`). Only the awareness-mantissa retired — empirically oversized (D-MTS-6 GREEN, k\*=1, 2 bits/edge). Reasoning is now the **V3 substrate**: thinking-engine runs it, cognitive-shader-driver dispatches over MailboxSoA, p64 converges (call-site shim), the P4 ancestry pipeline wires awareness into **facet tenants** (autopoiesis triangle + `SpoFacet`), not a wider edge.

Honesty flags: Gemini long-context = **complement** not substitute (corrected); graphrag-rs perf figures are self-reported; "MIT causality learning" has no single canonical proposal (Uhler observational-disentanglement is best graph-causality fit). v3 gems landed: ENTROPY-MILESTONES M20/M9, le-contract §3 [H], FUTURE-DESIGN (`CascadeChannels8` = confirmed NEXT wiring edge), COMPONENT-MAP (p64-bridge = call-site pattern, not a dep). graphrag-rs is external (automataIA), REUSE-as-reference only; PersonalAI (2506.17001) is the closest sibling (thesis hyper-edge + 4 retrievers, all LLM-maintained — we are the no-LLM inversion).

## 2026-07-18 — E-WI3-CONTINUOUS-FIELD-ANALYTIC-1 — WI-3 resolved: the monotone-bounded continuous-field exit is ANALYTIC (helix RollingFloor at 1 byte), not the materialized ladder

**Status:** FINDING (PROBE-HELIX-CONTINUOUS-FIELD measured, main-thread-adjudicated; resolves WI-3 of `E-BGZ-TENSOR-LANE-REVIEW-1` axis (c) — the one open work-item that could move a lane assignment).
**What:** the honourable-mention (`E-PALETTE-RESIDUAL-LADDER-1` / le-contract §3) assigned the continuous-field exit (geo-elevation) to the **materialized** bgz-tensor ladder. WI-3 measured the **analytic** helix alternative: `RollingFloor::quantize`→`bucket_center` reconstructs a synthetic ~1500 m elevation field (linear / gamma / 256-terraced, N=1024) at **1 byte / RMSE ≈ 1.7 m = 0.11 % of range**, reproducing the 5.86 m = 1500/256 terrace figure EXACTLY; a composable 2-byte stacked `RollingFloor` reaches ≈ 0.023 m terraces. PASS-for-analytic (WI-3 band: within ~0.5 % of range at ≤ 6 bytes — cleared at 1 byte). **Ruling:** the **monotone-bounded** continuous-field exit is analytic (helix `RollingFloor` palette256 + stacked floors), so the materialized ladder demarcates to its distinct lane — **unbounded / clustered / multi-modal** value distributions (the heavy-tailed weight rows the CLAM/centroid machinery is built for) that don't fit a single bounded floor range. Consistent with the overall lane-review "different workloads" demarcation.
**Honest caveat:** the shipped multi-byte residue codecs (`ResidueEdge`/`Signed360`) are sphere/place codecs, NOT scalar-field codecs — "analytic" here is `RollingFloor` (1 byte) + a stacked-floor composition built from shipped public API, not a residue.rs multi-byte scalar codec.
**Cross-ref:** `E-BGZ-TENSOR-LANE-REVIEW-1` (WI-3 was its one lane-moving open item), `E-PALETTE-RESIDUAL-LADDER-1` (the honoured ladder, now demarcated to unbounded/clustered), `E-FISHERZ-CANONICAL-COSINE-REPLACEMENT-1` (helix = the analytic 2z rung), le-contract §3 honourable-mention (updated), TECH_DEBT WI-3 (paid). Probe: helix `continuous_field.rs` (`probe_helix_continuous_field`, deterministic, asserts structural sanity only).

## 2026-07-18 — E-BGZ-TENSOR-LANE-REVIEW-1 — bgz-tensor's adaptive codec reviewed against turbovec/PolarQuant/helix; all four axes DEMARCATE/KEEP, not retire; PROBE-WH-MAG-2 confirms the WH-escape-centroid pairing

**Status:** ENGINEERING-REVIEW FINDING (Opus reviewer, source-receipted; PROBE-WH-MAG-2 re-run this session; no refactor, no canon changed — TD-BGZ-TENSOR-PRE-LANE-REVIEW paid in part).
**Frame:** bgz-tensor predates turbovec/PolarQuant/helix; the review asked per axis consume-the-lane / demarcate / retire. Result: the adaptive codec occupies a lane the three newer primitives do NOT cover — **materialized place+residual RECONSTRUCTION of unbounded heavy-tailed WEIGHT ROWS for GEMM** — so every axis rules DEMARCATE or KEEP.
**(a) i4+i2 cascade vs turbovec Lloyd-Max/NativeLut — DEMARCATE (different workloads).** turbovec is a data-oblivious ANN SEARCH INDEX that decodes to ids and "trades the matmul away" (lance-graph-turbovec/lib.rs:49-53); the cascade reconstructs f32 rows (adaptive_codec.rs:311-341, cosine>0.9). Placements already split: turbovec = in-row 6×4-bit nibble; ladder = out-of-row (le-contract:216). Only overlap: the uniform i4/i2 leaf (ndarray quantized.rs:671,725) vs a Lloyd-Max leaf — a leaf swap (WI-1), not a lane consume.
**(b) hadamard_rotate vs PolarQuant rotation/QJL — DEMARCATE; mechanism CONFIRMED.** Re-ran PROBE-WH-MAG-2: bare WH NEUTRAL (0.929/1.317/1.869) but WH+escape+centroid — the SHIPPED pairing (encode() step 3 + Passthrough) — wins on structure (gradient+spike cenB/A 0.209, heavy-tailed escB/A 0.998, noise flat). WH survives because bgz-tensor never applies it bare. PolarQuant's QJL error-correction is displaced for the bounded/cosine lane by the analytic Fisher-z canon (E-FISHERZ); the two PolarQuant probes are unrun scaffolds (WI-2).
**(c) residual/location vs helix ResidueEdge/Signed360/CurveRuler — DEMARCATE (orthogonal place).** helix place = analytic/address-derived, regenerated from endpoints via the stride-4-over-17 walk, ZERO location storage (residue.rs:19-27, curve_ruler.rs:50-56, the Fisher-2z rung). bgz-tensor place = learned k-means centroid index, residual stored explicitly. helix cannot derive a k-means mean; bgz-tensor stores no regenerable phase. Genuine open overlap: the continuous-field geo exit (256 elevation terraces) is exactly helix's analytic-quantizer regime — WI-3 decides ownership.
**(d) Passthrough top-10% — KEEP (refuse-the-mold virtue, empirically load-bearing).** Stores exact f32 for high-LFD outliers that no fixed-width register fits; routing them to Signed360 (lossy) or any Lloyd-Max lane re-introduces the failure the tier escapes. E-CHESS needle discipline. Re-run proof: heavy-tailed 1.32→0.998 via escape alone. Only sub-question: escape predicate LFD vs L-inf (WI-4).
**Cross-ref:** TD-BGZ-TENSOR-PRE-LANE-REVIEW (paid-in-part), E-PALETTE-RESIDUAL-LADDER-1 (the honoured ladder), E-FISHERZ-CANONICAL-COSINE-REPLACEMENT-1 (QJL→Fisher-z displacement), E-H268-PROBE-WAVE-1-RESULTS (PROBE-WH-MAG bare-tile NEUTRAL), le-contract §3 honourable-mention + analytic-canon subsections, adaptive_codec.rs mod probe_wh_mag::probe_wh_mag_2. Open work-items WI-1..WI-4 in TECH_DEBT (none gating).

## 2026-07-16 — E-X265-PROBE-WAVE-2-RESULTS — two x265-replay conjectures measured: the sprite P-frame IS one helix code (Signed360 exact for helix-manifold motion, ResidueEdge-24bit insufficient); WH-magnitude TRANSFERS on structured tiles only, with the shipped escape+centroid pairing

**Status:** FINDING (two probes run against shipped primitives, deterministic seeds, Opus-adjudicated; plans `x265-sprite-replay-probe-v1.md` + the PROBE-WH-MAG-2 home named in `h268-probe-wave-v1.md`). No canon changed. E-SPRITE-IPB-HELIX-1 graded PASS-AT-SIGNED360 (scoped); E-WH-TWO-SIDES-SIG-CHECKSUM-1 leg 1 UPGRADED from bare NOT-TRANSFERRING to pairing-TRANSFERS-on-structured-tiles.

**P1 PROBE-SPRITE-REPLAY → PASS-AT-SIGNED360 (scoped) / ResidueEdge-24bit INSUFFICIENT. The KILL did NOT fire.** Object-level helix motion did NOT collapse into a dense per-splat MV field: one `Signed360` (48-bit) code per sprite per P-frame reconstructs P-anchor motion AND parametric B-frames EXACTLY — mean/max 0.0 world-units, B-frame bidir-delta 0.0, over N=8 sprites × 6 anchors (I+5P) × 2 B-frames/gap. **HONEST CAVEAT (documented in `sprite_replay.rs:38-52`):** ground truth is drawn from helix's OWN `signed_lift` hemisphere template, so the 0.0 result proves CAPACITY (16-bit `azimuth` trivially indexes n∈0..240) + ROUND-TRIP (the hand-built inverse recovers n and sign exactly) + SIGN carriage — NOT that arbitrary independently-captured object motion fits the helix manifold. So "P-frame = one Signed360 code per sprite" is FEASIBLE at 48-bit **for helix-manifold motion** [proven]; arbitrary-motion generality stays [H], gated on a probe with independent ground truth. **ResidueEdge (24-bit) is INSUFFICIENT for signed motion on two independent axes:** (1) hemisphere-blind — only the unsigned upper-hemisphere pipeline exists (no sign bit), so `Sign::Neg` sprites reconstruct to the wrong hemisphere: mean 16.4 / max 42.4 world-units; (2) rank-adjacency hazard — even correct-hemisphere `Sign::Pos` sprites err (mean 3.55 / max 17.6) because n is squeezed through the coarse 8-bit `end_idx` bucket and a ±1 rank error, under golden-spiral ~137.5° rank spacing, lands a spatially distant point. **Ruling: the sprite motion primitive should be Signed360-only.** ResidueEdge-24bit is a residue-edge codec, not a motion code — the mismatch is structural, not a tuning gap.

**P2 PROBE-WH-MAG-2 → the WH-magnitude leg UPGRADES from bare NOT-TRANSFERRING to TRANSFERS-ON-STRUCTURED-TILES with the shipped pairing.** WH-MAG-1 (E-H268-PROBE-WAVE-1-RESULTS) closed the *bare-tile* leg NOT-TRANSFERRING (B/A 0.929/1.317/1.869). WH-MAG-2 pairs WH with the two things the shipped row codec (E-PALETTE-RESIDUAL-LADDER-1) always pairs it with — a passthrough ESCAPE tier (top-10% by L-inf hardness, esc_frac 0.098) and a CENTROID RESIDUAL — on the SAME seeded 16-cell corpus. Result (256 tiles/class): gradient+spike escB/A **0.815** + cenB/A **0.209** BOTH clear the <0.9 PASS bar → the pairing TRANSFERS on structured tiles; heavy-tailed escB/A **0.998** (escape only masks WH's damage to parity) + cenB/A **1.232** (centroid ≈ 0 for zero-mean+outliers, so it HURTS this class); uniform-noise escB/A **1.66** + cenB/A **1.66** (no transfer). `bareB/A` reproduced 0.929/1.317/1.869 as a same-run cross-check. **CAVEAT (audit): the escB/A and cenB/A columns are COMPOUND — Path A has no escape/centroid sibling, so they measure "shipped pairing vs direct-quantize-everything," NOT WH's isolated marginal value.** `bareB/A` is the only clean WH isolation and stays NEUTRAL; WH's standalone worth beyond escape+centroid is unmeasured. No invalid MSE deflation (passthrough = exact storage; in-sample centroid mirrors the shipped stored-codebook accounting) — but the win is a property of the PAIRING, not WH alone. The centroid is CLASS-DEPENDENT: heavy lifting on structured (0.209), harmful on heavy-tailed zero-mean+outliers (1.232 > 0.998).

**Consequence:** sprite amortization stands at the Signed360 width for helix-manifold motion (arbitrary-motion probe named, deferred); the WH-magnitude adoption is greenlit ONLY as the escape+centroid PAIRING on structured tiles (the shipped row codec is already exactly this — validated, not new machinery). Neither result changes canon; the two-algebra rule and I-SUBSTRATE-MARKOV are untouched.

**Cross-ref:** E-SPRITE-IPB-HELIX-1 (graded PASS-AT-SIGNED360 scoped, ResidueEdge-24bit insufficient), E-WH-TWO-SIDES-SIG-CHECKSUM-1 (leg 1 upgraded), E-H268-PROBE-WAVE-1-RESULTS (WH-MAG-1 bare NEUTRAL — the predecessor), E-PALETTE-RESIDUAL-LADDER-1 (the shipped WH+escape+centroid pairing this validates), helix `ResidueEdge`/`Signed360` (residue.rs — 24-bit end_idx vs 48-bit azimuth+polar), ndarray `pr-x12-h268-morton-wgpu-synergies.md` §5 wave-2 results sub-table + §10 WH-leg RESULT amendment, plans `x265-sprite-replay-probe-v1.md` §Results. Probes: `crates/helix/src/sprite_replay.rs` (`probe_sprite_replay_core`), `crates/bgz-tensor/src/adaptive_codec.rs` mod `probe_wh_mag::probe_wh_mag_2` — both deterministic (SplitMix64), assert structural sanity only, no verdict.

## 2026-07-18 — E-GRAPHRAG-REPRESENTATIONS-INVENTORY-1 — 7 GraphRAG papers map cleanly onto the V3 substrate; the crate is "AriGraph in name, RAG in retrieval"; the retrieval keystone (RRF) and the causality-trajectory wires are one-function-short gaps

**Status:** SYNTHESIS (2026-07-18, `.claude/knowledge/graphrag-representations-inventory.md`). Grounded — paper briefs from 6 Opus readers + MDPI; substrate rows source-verified against `canonical_node.rs`/`class_view.rs`/`facet.rs`/`ocr.rs`. Honesty flags [G]/[H]/proposed carried through.

**The finding:** all 7 papers (AriGraph, PersonalAI, Document-GraphRAG, SAP-Practical, StepChain, GraphRAG-FI, GraphRAG-under-Fire) cluster onto existing V3 carriers — none needs a new layer. (1) **AriGraph fidelity gap:** the `arigraph/` crate has the update-rule + agnostic-graph but runs a *parallel Hamming top-k* for episodic recall = the RAG baseline AriGraph beats; the first-class episodic edge (`Ee` / the unwired `EpisodicEdges64`) + the chained semantic→episodic search (their Eq.1) are MISSING. (2) **The retrieval keystone (RRF fusion) is one free fn short** — every ranked leg (BM25/PPR/CAM-PQ) exists, no combinator; the G0 harness compares by rank position instead of fusing. (3) **Provenance→trust is unwired** — `WitnessCorpus.source_url` exists but never reaches `TruthValue.confidence` (the only defense that works vs GRAGPOISON, and load-bearing for OCR mixed-trust docs regardless of adversary). (4) **GraphRAG-FI + PersonalAI independently re-derive** the workspace's MUL/free-energy meta-uncertainty and preserve-contradictions stances — validation, not novelty. (5) **PersonalAI's `thesis` vertex** (per-proposition grouping, their most load-bearing memory type) is a third partition the workspace lacks — a community-within-episode, parallel to `EpisodicBasins`.
